Today, injection molding of composites with modern preparations, super-anatomic clear matrices, and heated multi-viscosity composite can solve so many problems without resorting to crowns. In this fast moving and entertaining lecture Dr. Clark will demonstrate the art and science of monolithic injection molded composite dentistry.Learning Objectives: The 6 keys to world class contacts 100% of the time in a Class II.How to injection mold to treat black triangles, peg laterals, fractured teeth and diastemas.How to safely warm composite, and why.Deep margin management.Ultra glossy stain proof anterior restorations.

title: Contemporary Periodontal Care: Practical Strategies for Diagnosis, Treatment & Maintenance - Part 1
description: In 2018 the American Academy of Periodontology published the updated Classification for Periodontal and Peri-implant Diseases and Conditions. This lecture will explore the updated definition of periodontal disease diagnosis and classification. Current use of relevant clinical parameters and patient assessment will be reviewed. Treatment options including traditional resective, regenerative, and innovative laser-based methods will be presented. Clinical cases and short video clips will be used to illustrate these principles. Maintenance strategies to prevent and manage the periodontal and implant patient will also be discussed.Learning Objectives: Become familiar with current concepts of Periodontal and Peri-implant disease conditions including diagnoses, etiologies, and clinical implications.Learn important components of the range of conservative to advanced surgical treatment options for the most common periodontal diseases encountered in clinical practice.Learn and be familiar with strategies for maintenance of the periodontal patient.

title: Effective Patient Communication - Part 1
description: This session emphasizes the crucial role of communication in fostering patient trust and delivering top-notch care. Attendees will discover methods to address patient concerns, explain procedures clearly, and improve overall patient satisfaction.Learning Objectives: Utilize active listening skills to effectively respond to patient concerns.Simplify complex dental procedures for better patient understanding. Create strategies for handling challenging conversations with patients.

title: Endodontic Diagnosis & Emergency Treatment: The Initial Skills That Will Set You Apart
description: This 90-minute intensive will teach the restorative dentist to transform Endodontic Diagnosis from the mystery that it often is to a truly mastery level skill. Here is a story that often manifests in the dental practice: Imagine in the theatre of your mind that you are preparing six maxillary anterior veneers for your favorite patient and with your favorite dental assistant and the treatment is prepaid. Here is your chance to thoroughly enjoy your most desirable kind of dentistry without interruptions while giving huge value to your patient. Only a big problem shows up…during the six preps things dramatically change your positive experience. An emergency patient walks into your office with a severely swollen face demanding to see “The Doctor” as he has been up all night and now is having trouble breathing. How do you solve this situation accurately and efficiently and effectively and still have your favorite patient continuing to feel she has your full attention. This diagnosis and emergency skill is underperformed in almost all restorative practices. Until now! The real benefit to you and your patients from learning John’s immediately applicable Endodontic Diagnosis and Emergency Flowchart will help you grow your practice by allowing you to continuing your restorative day while simultaneously gaining trust and appreciation of perhaps a new patient, who, in this story, just might be from a large family of 37 siblings and cousins and they are all depending on their toothache brother to find them a new “Good Dentist.” Here is the opportunity for you to be that GOOD DENTIST who says “come right over” to the patient in pain.Learning Objectives: Learn and immediately apply the Six Critical Clinical Distinctions of Endodontic Diagnosis. Learn how to efficiently and effectively administer the Dozen Clinical Tests that allow you to duplicate the symptom and PROVE the diagnosis which then dictates the emergency treatment plan.Diagnose the $250K undetected and illusive “Crack

title: Ergonomics, Wellness & Efficiency for the Entire Dental Team - Part 1
description: Dentistry is a mix of engineering, ergonomics, art and acrobatics. Many practitioners have made many updates and adjustments to their practices. Despite those, many professionals have not added improvements to their self-awareness and self-care. As a result, there continues to be a high incidence and prevalence of musculoskeletal pain in the dental community. This presentation will provide an overview to the state of dental practice and share several strategies to counteract the trauma of dental practice on the practitioner. Please come, listen and share your story while taking home some invaluable tips for a more efficient, comfortable practice.Learning Objectives: Define the terms posture, body mechanics and ergonomics.Discuss musculoskeletal disorders, cumulative trauma and pain.Discuss the characteristics of operator/assistance stools, patient chairs, office design and optimal function.Discuss lighting, magnification, instrumentation and technique.Identify activities, techniques and prevention strategies to reduce stress in the operatory.Discuss the positive benefits of regular exercise for the dental professional.Discuss conservative pain management for back and neck pain in dentistry: Mechanical Diagnosis & Therapy.
instructors: Timothy Caruso, PT, MBA, MS, CERT.MDT, CEAS

title: How to Stop a Coccus - Part 1
description: Dry mouth and medication induced xerostomia present incredible challenges for restorative success. Can we really expect our restorations to withstand an enemy so small it can’t be seen with the naked human eye, armed with secretions capable of disintegrating the hardest biological substance on earth? If you’re salivating to break free of nylon bristles and string, you’ll be pleasantly surprised the modern anti-caries arsenal has more than just the same ol’ electronegative anion in a new fun flavor.Learning Objectives: Compare remineralization agents based on mechanisms of action.Identify the “stealthy extreme” caries risk patients.Produce a more favorable ecologic balance within the mouth.Modify patient behavior and improve treatment prognosis.

title: Invisalign Integration & Team Training Part 1: Consultation Workflow & Case Acceptance
description: This is an all-day, four-part course (Parts 1-4). Part 1: Consultation Workflow and Case Acceptance.This course will teach how to treatment plan Invisalign, case selection, case acceptance, how to get patients to say yes, and how to grow your practice through digital technology and iTero.Learning Objectives: Understand how to diagnose Invisalign cases.Feel confident utilizing digital technology with the iTero scanner in all aspects of the practice.Learn how to treatment plan effectively to get patients to say "yes."

title: Navigate CBCT Territory with Confidence: When to Diagnose & When to Refer
description:
Not every lesion is obvious and not every shadow is pathology. This interactive, case-based lecture highlights radiographic ‘red flags’ in 2D and CBCT imaging that every dentist must recognize. Through carefully selected clinical examples, the session emphasizes diagnostic cues that may impact treatment planning and patient outcomes.Learning Objectives: Recognize normal anatomic variations and key radiographic features of pathologic findings on CBCT to distinguish between benign and clinically significant abnormalities.Apply a structured diagnostic strategy to evaluate CBCT scans efficiently and accurately across different clinical scenarios.Determine when findings fall within the clinician’s scope of practice versus when referral to an oral and maxillofacial radiologist or medical specialist is indicated.Communicate radiographic findings and referral rationales clearly to patients and interdisciplinary colleagues to ensure optimal diagnostic and treatment outcomes.
.
instructors: Setarah Lavasani, DDS, MS, FICD, FGDIA

title: Recognizing Red Flags of Myofunctional Disorders
description: Early detection is key to preventing long-term oral, facial, and airway dysfunction. This course helps participants identify the most common red flags of myofunctional disorders—such as mouth breathing, tongue posture issues, speech difficulties, and sleep disturbances—and understand their impact on growth, development, and overall health. Gain practical tools to recognize dysfunction early and guide patients toward proper intervention.Learning Objectives: Identify the key clinical signs and symptoms associated with myofunctional disorders across different age groups.Understand how oral posture, muscle function, and breathing patterns impact craniofacial growth and airway development.Recognize the connection between myofunctional dysfunction and related issues such as sleep-disordered breathing and orthodontic relapse.Differentiate between normal and dysfunctional oral habits to support early detection and appropriate referral.Develop strategies for effective interdisciplinary collaboration to guide patients toward comprehensive myofunctional and airway-focused care.

title: Social Media for Dental Practices: From Engagement to Conversion
description: Are you spending money on Social Media Marketing, but not seeing any conversions? Feeling overwhelmed and don’t know what to post for engagement? Are you falling behind your competition? Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok—where should you begin, and how can you use social media to grow your practice? Join us for an engaging course on mastering social media, complete with hands-on training for Ads and capturing video testimonials (scripts included!). In this interactive workshop-style course designed for dental practices by the Marketologist™ herself, Minal, you’ll learn how to build an effective social media presence, navigate algorithms with confidence, and create posts that highlight your unique services. Discover how to craft Ads that attract new patients, train your team to capture compelling patient testimonials, and maximize the impact of video content. Minal will also share time-saving tools, copywriting tips, and strategies to engage current patients, strengthen your brand, and grow your practice.Learning Objectives: Learn what, when, and how to use social media for practice marketing.Gain actionable insights and implementation tools that you can use immediately to improve your social media pages.Discover the latest tactics and trends, including video and content redistribution.Learn how to create targeted social media ads.Learn how to repurpose social content to maximize your social media efforts.

title: The Biofilm Breakthrough: Using a Scientific Approach to Evict Uninvited Guests
description: Have you ever wondered how periodontal therapy can influence systemic inflammation beyond just improving oral health? What role does biofilm play in periodontal disease, and why is it more significant than dental calculus? How can advancements in air polishing technology revolutionize your approach to biofilm management?Explore these compelling questions and more in this comprehensive course, designed to equip you with cutting-edge strategies for enhancing periodontal treatment protocols.Emerging research reveals that periodontal therapy can significantly reduce systemic inflammation, prompting dental professionals to expand their focus beyond oral health. Biofilm, rather than calculus, is the primary cause of periodontal disease. This course examines two decades of biofilm research and advancements in air polishing technology.Participants will learn about current methodologies for managing biofilm and calculus using lasers, ultrasonics, hand instruments, and air polishers. They will also assess which tools are best for disrupting biofilm and which are more effective for removing calculus. Attendees will leave with advanced strategies to enhance their periodontal treatment protocols.Learning Objectives: Assess the impact of periodontal therapy on systemic inflammation.Identify biofilm's role as the primary factor in periodontal disease.Apply advancements in air polishing technology for effective biofilm management.Evaluate the effectiveness of various tools for biofilm disruption and calculus removal.Implement advanced strategies to enhance periodontal treatment protocols.
instructors: Joy Void-Holmes, DHS, BSDH, RDH, FADHA, AADH

title: The Changing Epidemiology of Oral Cavity Cancer: An Update
description: This presentation will discuss the changing epidemiology of oral cavity cancer, with reference to the emerging epidemic of oral tongue cancer in young adults. It will highlight novel and emerging risk factors including poor oral hygiene, microbiome, sugar-sweetened beverages, and diet. In addition, a discussion regarding differing risk profiles and risk modification between different ages, races, and sexes.Learning Objectives: Describe the changing epidemiology of oral cavity cancer in the US and globally.Discuss recurrence and survival in early-onset and average-onset oral cavity cancer.Discuss emerging etiopathologies for early-onset and average-onset oral cavity cancer.Differentiate risk profiles for oral cavity cancer between different ages, races, and sexes.Identify challenges in treatment of oral cavity cancer in young adults and barriers to further investigation.

title: Utilizing 3D Printing Technology in General Dentistry
description: The opportunities for 3D printing and scanning and integration into other dental office systems like Cone Beam CT imaging is expanding at an alarming rate. This course will discuss the practical ways to incorporate this technology into the general dental office and the tremendous potential that it has for Return on Investment (ROI). Topics will include 3D printed study models, surgical guides, in-oﬃce aligners and occlusal guards. Particular emphasis will be placed on the workﬂow that team members can support.Learning Objectives: Understand the current printing technology and choose the best option for your practice.Explore opportunities for 3D printing in general dentistry.Create a printing workﬂow that has a strong ROI.Utilize the skills of your team to perform digital tasks.

title: A Gum-azing Journey with Gingival Grafting
description: Gum recession is a common and often challenging condition that not only affects a patient's smile but can also lead to increased sensitivity and root decay. This lecture, "A Gum-azing Journey with Gingival Grafting," will provide general dentists and hygienists with a comprehensive and engaging look at how to effectively diagnose and treat these cases. Join Dr. Setareh Enghiad, a dually board-certified periodontist, as she demystifies the principles and practical applications of gingival grafting. Dr. Enghiad will guide you through the most common techniques, from initial patient selection to final post operative care. Through valuable clinical insights and a step-by-step video of a free gingival graft procedure, you will gain a clear understanding of the indications, contraindications, and best practices for achieving predictable and aesthetically pleasing results. This session is designed to empower you with the knowledge to identify and discuss these procedures with your patients, improving their oral health and restoring their confidence. Attendees will leave with a greater understanding of how to incorporate gingival grafting principles into their practice, enhancing their skills and expanding their scope of care.Learning Objectives: Identify the key indications and contraindications for common gingival grafting techniques.Review the step-by-step clinical procedure for a free gingival graft.Understand how to effectively communicate the benefits and process of gingival grafting to patients.Formulate evidence-based answers to the five most common patient questions regarding soft tissue grafting procedure, risks, and recovery.

title: Vital Pulp Therapy: The Old, The New & The Future
description: Vital pulp therapy (VPT) plays a crucial role in managing mature permanent teeth with pulp exposure or inflammation, offering a less invasive alternative to root canal treatment. The decision on when and how to perform VPT depends on the degree of pulp involvement, the tooth's vitality, and clinical symptoms. Key VPT options include direct pulp capping, partial pulpotomy, and full pulpotomy, each with specific indications based on the extent of pulpal inflammation. Recent advancements in biomaterials, mineral trioxide aggregate and other bioceramic materials, have improved treatment outcomes by promoting pulp healing and reducing the risk of failure. Hence, these treatment modalities are emerging as promising treatments for maintaining tooth vitality. Proper case selection, strict asepsis, using biocompatible materials and good coronal seal are mandatory for success. This presentation reviews the current VPT options, emphasizing the importance of diagnosis, material selection, and individualized treatment planning to achieve optimal long-term outcomes in younger and adult patients.Learning Objectives: Recognize the cases where VPT can be performed.Have the knowledge about the materials used for VPT.Have knowledge about the procedure.
instructors: Avina Paranjpe, BDS, MS, MSD, PhD

title: All TMJ Patients are NUTS, Right? How to Evaluate, Diagnosis & Treat the Most Common TMJ Problems - Part 2
description: TMJ disorders are one of those areas where almost every dentist feels uncomfortable. In dental school we didn’t get much classroom information, and little or no clinical experience. Our treatments in practice are often hit and miss, without clarity on why sometimes we hit, and other times we miss. In this lecture Dr. Spencer will review the relevant anatomy and discuss simple examination, diagnosis and treatment techniques that will allow your attendees to STUN their next TMD patients with their understanding of the patient's issues, and allow them to help many more people. Learning Objectives: A review or the relevant anatomy of the head and neck.Understand basic principles of focused examination and evaluation for TMD.Have a straight forward way to diagnose the most common TM disorders.Understand the treatments for the most common TM disorders.

title: Becoming A Special Needs Warrior: Equipping Yourself to Treat Patients With Special Needs - Part 2
description: It doesn’t matter your experience or training; everyone has questions and fears when it comes to treating patients with special needs. Dr. Dent breaks down these common barriers for dental providers by equipping you with the knowledge and how-to in a fun and practical way that will build your confidence and empower you to be a special needs warrior in your practice.Learning Objectives: List treatment options available for special needs patients and their pros and cons.Demonstrate alternative solutions to medical immobilization protective stabilization.Discuss minimally invasive treatment options.

title: Better Composite Restorations - Part 2
description: Until now, our composite went “in” the tooth. Now we can go “around” the tooth. Today, injection molding of composites with modern preparations, super-anatomic clear matrices, and heated multi-viscosity composite can solve so many problems without resorting to crowns. In this fast moving and entertaining lecture Dr. Clark will demonstrate the art and science of monolithic injection molded composite dentistry.Learning Objectives: The 6 keys to world class contacts 100% of the time in a Class II.How to injection mold to treat black triangles, peg laterals, fractured teeth and diastemas.How to safely warm composite, and why.Deep margin management.Ultra glossy stain proof anterior restorations.

title: CBCT 101: Case-Based Clinical Applications, Signs of Important Pathology & The Radiation Risk “Talk”
description: This interactive, case-based session introduces the applications of CBCT across various dental disciplines including implant planning, endodontics, TMJ evaluation, oral surgery, orthodontics and maxillary sinus evaluation. Attendees will learn to recognize radiographic signs of significant pathology, and gain practical guidance on communicating radiation risk to patients with confidence and clarity. The course bridges science, safety, and clinical application to help practitioners make informed, patient-centered diagnostic decisions.Learning Objectives: Recognize the clinical scenarios where 3D imaging adds value to clinical diagnosis and decision making.Become familiar with radiographic signs of non-odontogenic bone loss and their differential diagnosis.Be able to understand and communicate the radiation dose related to CBCT imaging and its comparison with daily background radiation.
instructors: Setarah Lavasani, DDS, MS, FICD, FGDIA

title: Commercial Real Estate Strategies for Dental Practices: Startups, Expansions & Relocations
description: This session offers a comprehensive overview for dentists who are considering starting a dental office, expanding an existing dental practice, or relocating to a new location.With combined experience of over 40 years in commercial real estate, Steve Kikikis and Ateah Wright are regarded as the go-to experts in the Pacific Northwest for commercial healthcare real estate. They will share their insights on the pros and cons of starting your own location, how to efficiently expand your current practice, and what to consider when purchasing a building for relocation.You’ll learn how to assemble and coordinate your professional team—including your contractor, broker, banker, and attorney—and gain valuable tips for evaluating and securing property, whether you’re buying or working with a landlord. This session will provide practical guidance on avoiding costly mistakes that can save you thousands of dollars and months of time.Learning Objectives: Understand how to navigate the commercial real estate landscape in 2026, whether leasing space for a startup, expanding your current office, or purchasing a building for your next practice.Learn how to identify and secure prime locations, evaluate when it’s the right time to buy versus lease, and understand the key elements of a strong lease agreement.Discover how to manage your landlord instead of letting them manage you, and gain strategies to protect your interests throughout your tenancy or purchase.

title: Conquer Instagram: “Reeling” Into the Now
description: Do you want to leverage Instagram for your practice? With over a billion active users, Instagram isn’t just a platform for selfies and food pics—it’s a powerful marketing tool for businesses, including dental practices. Instagram is an effective way to connect with current and potential patients, educate them about your services, and turn new leads into loyal followers.This engaging and interactive workshop offers valuable insights, creative strategies, and plenty of laughs. Minal will teach you how to build content with posts, stories, and reels that showcase your practice's personality and connect with your audience.Discover how to educate, entertain, inspire and run Ads while keeping your content fresh and exciting. From crafting engaging captions to leveraging analytics, Minal will share practical tips to elevate your Instagram game. Bonus: Posts, Reels & Stories Ideas included!Learning Objectives: Learn how to leverage Instagram for your practice.Understand how to craft engaging posts, stories, and reels that resonate with your audience.Gain insider tips and tricks for maximizing your reach and building your brand on Instagram.

title: Contemporary Periodontal Care: Practical Strategies for Diagnosis, Treatment & Maintenance - Part 2
description: In 2018 the American Academy of Periodontology published the updated Classification for Periodontal and Peri-implant Diseases and Conditions. This lecture will explore the updated definition of periodontal disease diagnosis and classification. Current use of relevant clinical parameters and patient assessment will be reviewed. Treatment options including traditional resective, regenerative, and innovative laser-based methods will be presented. Clinical cases and short video clips will be used to illustrate these principles. Maintenance strategies to prevent and manage the periodontal and implant patient will also be discussed.Learning Objectives: Become familiar with current concepts of Periodontal and Peri-implant disease conditions including diagnoses, etiologies, and clinical implications.Learn important components of the range of conservative to advanced surgical treatment options for the most common periodontal diseases encountered in clinical practice.Learn and be familiar with strategies for maintenance of the periodontal patient.

title: Dental Service Organizations & Their Impact on Dentistry in Washington State
description: This presentation will address state and federal laws and regulations governing dental service organizations (“DSO”) arrangement as well as practical considerations for dentists and dental practices considering entering into DSO arrangements, including pros and cons of DSO arrangements and contracting and negotiating tips when entering into DSO arrangements.Learning Objectives: Legal and regulatory considerations related to DSO arrangements.Pros and cons of DSO arrangements.Tips for contracting and negotiating with DSOs.

title: Effective Patient Communication - Part 2
description: This session emphasizes the crucial role of communication in fostering patient trust and delivering top-notch care. Attendees will discover methods to address patient concerns, explain procedures clearly, and improve overall patient satisfaction.Learning Objectives: Utilize active listening skills to effectively respond to patient concerns.Simplify complex dental procedures for better patient understanding. Create strategies for handling challenging conversations with patients.

title: Ergonomics, Wellness & Efficiency for the Entire Dental Team - Part 2
description: Dentistry is a mix of engineering, ergonomics, art and acrobatics. Many practitioners have made many updates and adjustments to their practices. Despite those, many professionals have not added improvements to their self-awareness and self-care. As a result, there continues to be a high incidence and prevalence of musculoskeletal pain in the dental community. This presentation will provide an overview to the state of dental practice and share several strategies to counteract the trauma of dental practice on the practitioner. Please come, listen and share your story while taking home some invaluable tips for a more efficient, comfortable practice.Learning Objectives: Define the terms posture, body mechanics and ergonomics.Discuss musculoskeletal disorders, cumulative trauma and pain.Discuss the characteristics of operator/assistance stools, patient chairs, office design and optimal function.Discuss lighting, magnification, instrumentation and technique.Identify activities, techniques and prevention strategies to reduce stress in the operatory.Discuss the positive benefits of regular exercise for the dental professional.Discuss conservative pain management for back and neck pain in dentistry: Mechanical Diagnosis & Therapy.
instructors: Timothy Caruso, PT, MBA, MS, CERT.MDT, CEAS

title: Evidence-Based Care, Standard of Care, or Just Making It Up as We Go
description: How many times have you been told that planning the root glassy smooth is still the standard of care or that ultrasonics outperform hand instruments? Are fluoride alternatives genuinely as effective as traditional fluoride treatments, or is that just something you've heard? And what about manual toothbrushes—are they still holding their own, or have they been rendered obsolete by the rise of electric brushes? Who decides what’s right—your instructor, a trusted piece of scientific research, or your own clinical experience? Is your practice grounded in evidence-based care or are you stuck following outdated guidelines, or relying on guesswork?With the rise of social media and private online groups, oral health care professionals are increasingly turning to these platforms for advice and answers. Many share their opinions with absolute conviction, confidently claiming "evidence shows" or "this is the standard of care." But how often do we pause to ask—does the science back these claims?This course will challenge these assumptions as we explore the critical role of evidence-based dentistry (EBD) in dental hygiene. You’ll learn how to implement EBD principles in your practice, effectively share scientifically supported findings with your team, and stay informed as new discoveries reshape dental care.By the end of this course, you’ll have the tools to critically evaluate clinical recommendations and integrate proven, patient-centered strategies into your everyday care.Learning Objectives: Define evidence-based dentistry (EBD), standard of care, and best clinical practices, and explain the key differences between them.Identify sources of reliable evidence-based literature for dentistry and rank them based on credibility.Evaluate research findings and apply them effectively to clinical dental hygiene practices.Explore the standard of care for common dental hygiene procedures, supported by current literature.Develop strategies to integrate and share evidence-based practices
instructors: Joy Void-Holmes, DHS, BSDH, RDH, FADHA, AADH

title: How to Prepare an Endodontic Slidepath: The Secret of Making Mechanical Endodontics Predictable & Effortless
description: The most difficult part of the art of endodontics is “Finding” and “Following” root canal systems to length. Once dentists finally achieve reaching the radiographic terminus, the next big problem is preserving length control. John West will teach both the novice and mature endodontic clinician how to “Find” and “Follow” canals even when they are calcified, long, and curved! Understanding the reasons for Slidepath failure and the critical elements for Slidepath success will enable most dentists to treat their easy cases easier and, once understood and practiced, allow you to expand your level of treating to more difficult endodontic patient conditions such as posterior teeth. In this 90-minute learning, John teaches the marquis skill of solving the endodontic Slidepath block. Participants will also learn Manual and Mechanical techniques to prevent AND correct Blocks, Ledges, Transportations, and Perforations. If you want to invest in just ONE PNDC 2026 class, John promises that this is the one that will bring you the biggest ROI. Period!Learning Objectives: Learn the Four reasons you cannot reach the endodontic root canal system terminus.Learn when and how to use Mechanical vs. Manual Slidepath skills.Upon completion, participants will be able to master the Three Slidepath Motions.Know when the Slidepath is finished and how to prove it.

title: How to Stop a Coccus - Part 2
description: Dry mouth and medication induced xerostomia present incredible challenges for restorative success. Can we really expect our restorations to withstand an enemy so small it can’t be seen with the naked human eye, armed with secretions capable of disintegrating the hardest biological substance on earth? If you’re salivating to break free of nylon bristles and string, you’ll be pleasantly surprised the modern anti-caries arsenal has more than just the same ol’ electronegative anion in a new fun flavor.Learning Objectives: Compare remineralization agents based on mechanisms of action.Identify the “stealthy extreme” caries risk patients.Produce a more favorable ecologic balance within the mouth.Modify patient behavior and improve treatment prognosis.

title: Invisalign Integration & Team Training Part 2: Workflows
description: This is an all-day, four-part course (Parts 1-4). Part 2: Workflows. This course will teach the fully integrated workflow of Invisalign in your practice. This includes monitoring and tracking issues.Learning Objectives: Understand the steps of the workflow from start to finish in an Invisalign case.Identify the most common tracking issues.Learn how to monitor cases, including virtually.

title: Oral Cavity Cancer Reconstruction from A to Z
description: This presentation will discuss the surgical management of oral cavity cancers and reconstructive methods. It will highlight the evolution of healing following oral cavity cancer treatment.Learning Objectives: Review oral cavity cancer ablative surgical procedures.Discuss relevant oral cavity cancer reconstruction methods.Illustrate the evolution of reconstructed wounds throughout the head and neck cancer treatment journey.

title: The Power of the Tongue: How Myofunctional Therapy Supports Palatal Expansion & Optimal Growth
description: Palatal expansion is a critical component of airway-focused orthodontics, but without proper muscle function and oral posture, long-term stability and optimal outcomes can be compromised. This course explores the integral role of myofunctional therapy in supporting palatal expansion, guiding proper tongue posture, and ensuring functional harmony in orofacial development. Participants will gain a deeper understanding of the interplay between muscle function, craniofacial growth, and airway health—ultimately enhancing their ability to achieve better patient outcomes.Learning Objectives: Understand the Role of Myofunctional Therapy – Learn how proper tongue posture, nasal breathing, and orofacial muscle function impact palatal expansion and long-term stability.Recognize Key Myofunctional Disorders – Identify tongue ties, low tongue posture, and compensatory muscle patterns that can affect palatal development and treatment outcomes.Discover evidence-based strategies for incorporating myofunctional therapy alongside expansion appliances to improve patient compliance and.Develop a Functional Approach to Patient Care – Learn practical assessment tools and exercises that support palatal expansion, optimize oral function, and promote long-term orofacial balance.This session will provide clinicians with actionable strategies to enhance treatment success and empower patients with functional tools for lifelong health.

title: The Simple Approach to Occlusal Stability
description: The concepts of occlusion have most dentists confused. However, they really are simple. We just have to apply them appropriately for each patient. The most frequent questions posed by dentists include: Which teeth need to touch for stability? What bite (CR or IP) is appropriate for this patient? How do I know when to equilibrate? When is wear on teeth a problem? What do I do with the uneven edges of teeth? What do I do if the teeth are moving during function? This presentation will give you knowledge to easily plan and restore the “everyday” patients that are a large part of your practice. What’s more, you will be able to diagnosis and plan treatment with more ease and conﬁdence for those “troubling” cases that sit on your lab bench top.Learning Objectives: Learn the contributing factors to occlusal disease.When equilibration is important and when it is not.The essentials of diagnosis & treatment planning for achieving occlusal stability.When intercuspal position (IP) is the reasonable restorative position.

title: Decision-Making in Peri-implantitis: How to Treat, When to Treat, When to Explant
description: Peri-implantitis presents a significant challenge in implant dentistry due to its complex etiology and the unpredictability of treatment outcomes. Recent advancements in the field have introduced innovative technologies for implant surface decontamination, refined tissue manipulation techniques, and the application of artificial intelligence to predict clinical outcomes after treatment. This presentation integrates clinical observations, advanced surgical techniques, and the latest scientific evidence to enhance participants' understanding of peri-implantitis. Emphasis will be placed on therapeutic approaches, prognostic factors, and the transformative potential of artificial intelligence in improving treatment predictability and success.Learning Objectives: To understand the use of implant surface decontamination technologies.To apply the appropriate surgical approach based on case selection.To understand when an implant should be explanted versus when it should be treated for peri-implantitis.

title: JBLM AEGD Table Clinics
description: Post-graduate residents in the Joint Base Lewis-McChord (JBLM) Advanced Education in General Dentistry program will present table clinics. One CE credit available.Dr. Corinne Brown: Orthodontics for Predictable Prosthetic OutcomesOrthodontic interventions can play a critical role in preserving tooth structure, improving esthetics, and enhancing the function and longevity of prosthetic restorations. By carefully planning tooth movements before restorative treatment, dentists can minimize aggressive procedures and create predictable, patient-centered outcomes. Ultimately, starting with the right tooth in the right position allows us to provide durable, minimally invasive, and highly esthetic results.Dr. Makayla Medrano: Impact of Air Abrasion on Zirconia (3Y, 4Y, 5Y) Bonding4Y- and 5Y-PSZ are highly translucent zirconia ceramics that are utilized more for esthetic restorations, but their increased cubic phase composition separates them from the traditional 3Y-TZP. 3Y-TZP, containing increased tetragonal phase composition, is typically air abraded for increased mechanical strength and improved bonding. However, air abrasion produces mixed outcomes in 4Y- and 5Y-PSZ. While air abrasion can create mechanical retention on the bonding surface, it also can create microcracks in 4Y- and 5Y-PSZ creating a higher risk for fracture therefore decreasing mechanical strength in addition to limited transformational toughening. As a result, mechanical strength may increase in 3Y-TZP but decreases in 4Y- and 5Y-PSZ with air abrasion. Bonding, however, is clinically reliable, regardless of yttria content, with utilization of primers and cements with 10-MDP. This table clinic will present practical pearls for understanding yttria content impact on zirconia characteristics, selecting the proper air-abrasion protocol, and optimizing bonding strategies to improve restorative success.Dr. Matthew Melatti: Deep Margin Elevation: A Non-Surgical Alternative to Reposition the Finish LineDeep Ma

title: Cervical Spine, Skull Base & Sinuses, Oh My!
description: This course provides an overview of Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) and its role in dental diagnostics. Attendees will review basic principles of CBCT imaging and learn how to interpret common anatomical structures often seen in scans, including the cervical spine, skull base, and sinuses. The session will conclude with a case-based review to reinforce key concepts.Learning Objectives: Review Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) imaging and the basics of reading a CBCT scan.Understand the anatomy of the cervical spine, skull base, and sinuses.Recognize potential radiographic abnormalities in the cervical spine, skull base, and sinuses.

title: 30 Dental Marketing Strategies in 90 Minutes: Doable. Creative. Repeatable.
description: Fast-paced, actionable, and designed to spark your practice momentum!In just 90 minutes, you’ll discover 30 actionable strategies to boost patient engagement, connect with new patients, strengthen your practice’s brand, and drive growth.This fast-paced, idea-packed session is designed to give you a steady stream of innovative approaches to optimize internal, digital, community, and social media marketing. Say goodbye to wasted resources and hello to impactful, practical solutions that you can implement immediately.Whether you’re looking to energize your current efforts or explore fresh ideas, this presentation delivers the tools you need to stay ahead in today’s competitive dental landscape.Learning Objectives: Learn diverse dental marketing strategies, from traditional to digital, that will encourage practice growth.Gain the skills to acquire and utilize powerful video testimonials that showcase your practice's success.Build a marketing approach that’s sustainable, effective, and tailored to your practice.

title: Current Concepts in Peri-Implant Disease: Evidence-Based Diagnosis & Care
description: This lecture will address Peri-implant diseases, diagnoses, etiology and risk factors, treatment modalities, and maintenance strategies. The format will be lecture and case presentations, with video segments for illustrative purposes.Learning Objectives: Diagnose the various levels of peri-implant health and diseases.Discuss etiologies and risk factors associated with periiimplant disease.Understand the most current modalities of implant disease treatment.Learn and be able to implement conservative in-office maintenance strategies as well as counsel patients on best home care practices for dental implants.

title: Diagnosis & Treatment Planning in Today’s Dental Practice
description: Dentists and team members strive for simple and eﬀective strategies for diagnosis and treatment planning with their patients. However current challenges include the patient’s lack of awareness of their current health status and dependence on dental insurance. This course will review the esthetic, functional, structural and biological components of thorough hygiene and doctor exams with particular emphasis on clear patient communication and shared decision making, making “YES” a part of your patient response.Learning Objectives: Understand the essentials of diagnosis & treatment planning for achieving occlusal stability.Master the components of the collaborative oral health examination and discovery process.Engage your team to help facilitate the patient’s engagement during each and every visit to your practice.

title: DSO or Private Buyer? Making the Best Choice for Your Practice Sale
description: Selling your dental practice is one of the most significant decisions you’ll face. Beyond timing and price, you must decide who to sell to and how you’ll transition out. Over the past few years, corporate buyers—known as Dental Service Organizations (DSOs)—have become increasingly prominent, owning roughly 20% of dental practices.This session will compare selling to a DSO versus selling to a private buyer. You’ll learn the pros and cons to selling to a DSO vs. the advantages and disadvantages of selling to an individual. We’ll explore deal structures, work-back requirements, exit strategies, and DSO growth trends.We’ll also discuss what to expect when selling to an individual buyer who plans to own and operate the practice personally. Private sales often allow for more flexibility, personal connection, and control over how your patients and staff are transitioned. You’ll gain insight into financing options available to individual dentists, negotiation differences between private and corporate offers, and the long-term impacts on your legacy and practice culture.By the end of this session, you’ll have a clear understanding of the pros and cons of both types of buyers, helping you determine which path best fits your financial goals, transition timeline, and vision for your practice’s future.Learning Objectives: Understand the key differences between selling to a DSO and a private buyer.Explore common DSO deal structures, work-back terms, and exit strategies.Examine financing options available to private buyers and how they affect offers.Compare negotiation dynamics between DSO and private buyer transactions.Evaluate how each sale type impacts your staff, patients, and practice culture.Determine which buyer type best aligns with your financial and transition goals.
instructors:
Rodney Johnston, MBA, CMA
Megan Urban

title: Inclusive Dentistry: Building a Team & Practice for Neurodiversity
description: Dentistry is not just about teeth. It is about the person and their environment that can make or break the experience for a patient at the dentist. In an outside the box session, Dr. Dent offers alternatives for creating a sensory-friendly dental practice and team.Learning Objectives: Review all 8 senses.Discuss alternative options to adapt the dental environment to be more sensory friendly.Discuss non invasive dental treatment options and materials.

title: Invisalign Integration & Team Training Part 3: ClinChecks & 3D Controls
description: This is an all-day, four-part course (Parts 1-4). Part 3: ClinChecks and 3D Controls.We will dive deep into how to utilize each resource in the 3D control software. We will work on ClinChecks together and show how you can make any "crazy" ClinCheck into an easy and predictable case. We will also explore the latest advances and newest releases in the ClinCheck software.Learning Objectives: Understand how to efficiently utilize 3D Controls for every ClinCheck.Learn how to use the latest advances in the ClinCheck software.Feel confident with moving teeth predictably in the 3D Control software.

title: Navigate CBCT Territory with Confidence: When to Diagnose & When to Refer (Repeat)
description: Not every lesion is obvious and not every shadow is pathology. This interactive, case-based lecture highlights radiographic ‘red flags’ in 2D and CBCT imaging that every dentist must recognize. Through carefully selected clinical examples, the session emphasizes diagnostic cues that may impact treatment planning and patient outcomes.Learning Objectives: Recognize normal anatomic variations and key radiographic features of pathologic findings on CBCT to distinguish between benign and clinically significant abnormalities.Apply a structured diagnostic workflow to evaluate CBCT scans efficiently and accurately across different clinical scenarios.Determine when findings fall within the clinician’s scope of practice versus when referral to an oral and maxillofacial radiologist or medical specialist is indicated.Communicate radiographic findings and referral rationales clearly to patients and interdisciplinary colleagues to ensure optimal diagnostic and treatment outcomes.
instructors: Setarah Lavasani, DDS, MS, FICD, FGDIA

title: Obstructive Sleep Apnea: Looking Beyond the Teeth & Saving Lives! - Part 1
description: Dentists are uniquely positioned among healthcare providers to easily evaluate for signs of obstructive sleep apnea. Oral appliance therapy performed by a qualified dentist can also be an effective treatment, literally saving lives. In this lecture Dr. Spencer will review the basics of normal sleep, snoring and obstructive sleep apnea in adults and children. Your attendees will most likely be thinking that THEY and everyone they know must have sleep apnea before he is through! Don’t worry though, he will also cover diagnosis and treatment of obstructive sleep apnea, including the dentist’s role and appliance selection.This lecture includes the basics of normal sleep, snoring and sleep apnea in adults in children, and the dental professional's role in screening, referral and treatment of snoring and sleep apnea using oral appliances alone and in conjunction with CPAP as part of a multi-disciplinary team. The bruxism/sleep apnea connection will also be discussed, helping dentists see the relationship between so called "dental parafunction" and sleep apnea. Unlike many manufacturer based lectures, Dr. Spencer will review many of the most popular oral appliances and discuss pros and cons of each.Learning Objectives: Understand normal sleep and how to improve sleep.Understand how obstructive sleep apnea affects adults and children.Become familiar with the dental aspects of screening for and treating OSA.Understand possible problems and side effects of oral appliance therapy.

title: Offensive Dentistry - Part 1
description: Let’s face it, sitting through a lecture about dental caries or caries risk assessment isn’t appealing (in fact it sounds downright boring). However, the science of clinical cariology is beginning to have an impact on restorative techniques and technology. Streptococcus mutans has more to fear than a rheostat, now that we’re armed with salivary diagnostics and bioactive materials.Learning Objectives: Intervene medically in the caries process.Control patient caries risk with focused therapeutics.Design restorations to minimize recurrent decay.Improve the prognosis of restorative treatment.

title: Optimizing Tongue-Tie Release Surgery: Integrating Myofunctional Therapy for Enhanced Outcomes
description: Participants will discover how myofunctional therapy can transform surgical outcomes. This session highlights the essential role of pre- and post-surgical therapy in enhancing precision, speeding recovery, and reducing complications. Learn practical strategies to integrate therapy into comprehensive tongue-tie care—from preparation to long-term healing.Learning Objectives: Understand the benefits of myofunctional therapy in pre-surgical preparation.Discuss how myofunctional therapy can improve surgical precision and outcomes.Identify effective post-operative myofunctional exercises to support recovery.Recognize the signs of complications and how to mitigate them through therapy.Create a comprehensive care plan that includes myofunctional therapy in tongue-tie treatment.

title: Rotary vs. Reciprocation Endodontics: How Do I Choose?
description: This presentation will leave you with your own answers. While basics and principles are universal, all dentists have their own needs, their own skill sets, their own experience, and their own mix of endodontic patients. More than anyone, John understands this diversity and he will teach you knowledge for you to identify for yourself the skills needed for any manual and mechanical endodontic canal preparation. If endodontics is not yet FUN for you, your team, and your patients, John promises that after this 1.5-hour presentation, you know the skills to make Endo not only FUN but even the most FUN part of your dental day. After this class, when you see a 10:00 a.m. endodontic patient on your schedule, you will experience a growing sense of energy, newfound confidence, and relief. You can rarely receive this level of mastery anywhere else in the world in 90 minutes. So find your seat early, have some clean sheets of paper to take notes, and experience a shift in your endodontic future of control, competence, consistency, and confidence.Learning Objectives: Learn when and how to use Shapers and when and how to use Finishers.Know when to Finish Shaping, when to Fit the Cone.Know the proper post Shaping and pre-Packing 3D Irrigation Protocol.Choose simple and easy 3D Obturation.

title: Sensitive Teeth, Sensible Solutions: Managing Dentinal Hypersensitivity
description: Dentinal hypersensitivity (DH) is a common dental condition that affects a large majority of the population. Clinical management of dentinal hypersensitivity is possible.What do you use to help your patients? What is your go-to product? Are you using a scientific approach to treatment? While the choices for treating DH are endless, the materials that are used are not the same.This course will examine new product innovations for managing dentinal hypersensitivity using an evidenced-based approach for treatment options.Learning Objectives: Analyze the impact of dentinal hypersensitivity on diverse populations to understand its prevalence and etiology, enabling a broader understanding for improved dental practice.Improve diagnostic competence by accurately assessing, diagnosing, and recording dentinal hypersensitivity cases using a variety of diagnostic tools.Identify causative agents of dentinal hypersensitivity and apply strategies to mitigate their effects, ensuring a reduction in patient-reported sensitivity.Integrate new technological advancements in the treatment of dentinal hypersensitivity into clinical practice.
instructors: Joy Void-Holmes, DHS, BSDH, RDH, FADHA, AADH

title: Surviving Back & Neck Pain in Modern Dentistry: A User’s Guide for the Rest of Your Career! - Part 1
description: Is your career hazardous to your physical and mental health? Perhaps killing you slowly? Dentistry has come a long way in the last 150 years. The evolution of anesthesia, dental techniques, materials, equipment, and approaches has evolved yet daily discomfort continues to be tolerated day in and day out by many dental practitioners. Pain appears to be an accepted part of the dental profession, and it seems to be worsening. The patients are pain free but how about the dental staff?Is it possible for the modern day dental professional to counteract the ill-effects of their work on their bodies? Can injecting a dose of regular exercise and dynamic sitting into the operatory facilitate improved workflow with less pain? Is there a conservative approach to consider for pain relief prior to signing up for surgery? Perhaps Mechanical Diagnosis & Therapy is the answer for you.Are you someone who has a nagging pain in your neck or your back that no longer goes away? There is some numbness and tingling in your foot or your dominant hand is a bit clumsier than it used to be. Your sleep is disturbed, and you wake up with a headache. Advil and Motrin no longer “take care of it.” What are you going to do? Before going to the orthopedic/neurosurgeon for surgical intervention; before chronic becomes part of your vocabulary; before filing that disability claim, come and give this a listen!Learning Objectives: Discuss the incidence and prevalence of cervical/lumbar pain in the dental profession.Discuss pain generators in the cervical/lumbar regions.Discuss the unique musculoskeletal needs of the dental profession and the effects of prolonged static loading.Discuss conservative treatment options for cervical/lumbar pain.Discuss Mechanical Diagnosis & Therapy (McKenzie Method) to treatment of referred/radicular back and neck pain as an alternative to or screening prior to surgical intervention.Discuss chair side preventative postures, exercises and strategies.When to seek medical care.
instructors: Timothy Caruso, PT, MBA, MS, CERT.MDT, CEAS

title: The Changing Epidemiology of Oral Cavity Cancer: An Update (Repeat)
description: This presentation will discuss the changing epidemiology of oral cavity cancer, with reference to the emerging epidemic of oral tongue cancer in young adults. It will highlight novel and emerging risk factors including poor oral hygiene, microbiome, sugar-sweetened beverages, and diet. In addition, a discussion regarding differing risk profiles and risk modification between different ages, races, and sexes.Learning Objectives: Describe the changing epidemiology of oral cavity cancer in the US and globally.Discuss recurrence and survival in early-onset and average-onset oral cavity cancer.Discuss emerging etiopathologies for early-onset and average-onset oral cavity cancer.Differentiate risk profiles for oral cavity cancer between different ages, races, and sexes.Identify challenges in treatment of oral cavity cancer in young adults and barriers to further investigation.

title: The Great Tooth Killer: Cracked Teeth Prevention, Diagnosis & Treatment - Part 1
description: Currently the diagnosis and treatment of cracked and fractured teeth is largely symptom based and is therefore often a sad, end stage diagnosis. The fracturing process is one of dentistry’s last great mysteries and is poorly understood throughout the generalist and specialist dental community. In this engaging and entertaining lecture, you will learn to understand and diagnose pathologic cracks utilizing magnification and other modalities, and treat long before symptoms arise or the tooth becomes unsalvageable. Dr. Clark will teach routine identification of early cracks and teach the Clark Class II saucer shaped nonretentive preparation and Fissurotomy Class I that are a significant departure from the G.V. Black preparations that are a leading cause of cracked teeth. He will share his published approach to diagnosis based on 16X magnification, step by step predictable treatment, endodontic and periodontal implications, and when to extract.Learning Objectives: Have confidence identifying early tooth fracturing based on high level magnification.Learn why new Class II cavity preparations and other modalities are crucial to prevent eventual tooth fracture.Receive a blueprint for diagnosis and treatment of complete and incomplete fractures.Understand the Fissurotomy, Calla Lilly and Overlay preparations for Class I restoration.

title: Time Management & Organization - Part 1
description: Discover how to optimize daily operations and establish effective workflows in a dental practice. This course focuses on enhancing organizational skills, prioritizing tasks, and leveraging tools and technology to boost productivity.Learning Objectives: Recognize typical time-wasting activities and apply strategies to reduce them. Utilize organizational tools and methods to keep your workspace tidy.Organize and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines and alleviate workplace stress.

title: What Is the CDT Procedure Code for a Cleaning? Oh Wait! There IS No "Cleaning" Code!
description: Our profession is guilty of devaluing hygienists’ services by using the term “cleaning” to describe the preventive/therapeutic services they provide. Since there is no “cleaning” CDT procedure code, what is the procedure completed?Accurate, evidence-based diagnosis is the best method to determine appropriate treatment. Once this dental hygiene diagnosis is determined, the clinician will develop a treatment plan. This includes selecting the correct CDT procedure codes for the treatment. Too often, the procedure code selected may be inaccurate because dental software programs ONLY include the code number and title, NOT the descriptor.There are 7 distinct CDT procedure codes from which dentists and hygienists usually select for the services they provide for their patients, and each has a very distinct descriptor. Many clinicians are unaware of the ADA’s Current Dental Terminology (CDT) descriptors for each procedure code which must be used by the business staff to correctly bill patients and their insurance carriers. Inaccurate billing can often result in patients having to pay more out-of-pocket dollars then they were expecting to pay, which then can lead to mistrust. This can further lead to patients refusing to have ANY treatment completed because they cannot believe the “estimate” which they were given.As technology and treatment modalities evolve, new CDT procedure codes are developed, and current CDT procedure codes are amended by the ADA’s Code Maintenance Committee (which meets every March). This course will provide the latest nomenclature and descriptors for the Preventive/Perio codes (the 7 “cleaning” codes and more). Over the past 5 years, there has been a significant increase in procedure codes specific to preventive products used and recommended by hygienists. Now is the time to understand theseLearning Objectives: Explain the definitions and descriptors of the CDT Procedure Codes for Evaluations, Preventive and Periodontal Services, Implant Maintenance an
instructors: Kathy S. Forbes, RDH, BS, FADHA

title: Basic Life Support Training for Healthcare Providers | Hands-On Workshop ($95)
description: Attend this hands-on workshop to learn how to perform CPR and airway management and be better equipped to handle emergencies in your office and at home. Participants will receive their CPR certification course completion card (cards expire every two years).Learning Objectives: Be able to perform CPR and airway management.Be able to handle emergencies at work and home.Receive a CPR certification course completion card.

title: First Molar Extractions: Managing Poor Prognosis & How Orthodontics Can Reduce Future Restorations
description:
First molars with poor long-term prognosis present a common clinical challenge. This course will cover when first molar extractions may be the most strategic option and how orthodontic intervention can support optimal outcomes. We'll explore how coordinated treatment planning can reduce or even eliminate the need for complex future restorative work, improve occlusion, and enhance long-term oral health.Learning Objectives: Identify clinical indicators that suggest poor long-term prognosis for first molars and when extraction may be the most
appropriate treatment option.Recognize age-related timing considerations for optimal orthodontic intervention following molar extraction.Understand the role of orthodontic treatment in managing spaces resulting from first molar extractions to support
functional and esthetic outcomes.

title: 20 Team-Building Ideas in 90 Minutes
description: Looking to energize your team and elevate your practice’s culture?In this fast-paced, idea-packed session, you’ll discover 20 team-building ideas designed to enhance morale, improve communication, foster collaboration and build your practice brand.From creative activities to collaborative challenges, this session provides practical, actionable strategies to strengthen bonds among team members and boost productivity.Whether you’re looking to motivate your team or create a more cohesive workplace, these ideas are easy to implement and tailored for dental practices.Learning Objectives: Identify team-building ideas that boost morale, improve communication, foster collaboration, and reinforce the practice brand.Apply quick-win activities to lift morale and reinforce your practice culture in daily huddles and meetings.Learn about activities that connect a multigenerational dental team and strengthen day-to-day collaboration.

title: CBCT 101: Case-Based Clinical Applications, Signs of Important Pathology & The Radiation Risk “Talk” (Repeat)
description: This interactive, case-based session introduces the applications of CBCT across various dental disciplines including implant planning, endodontics, TMJ evaluation, oral surgery, orthodontics and maxillary sinus evaluation. Attendees will learn to recognize radiographic signs of significant pathology, and gain practical guidance on communicating radiation risk to patients with confidence and clarity. The course bridges science, safety, and clinical application to help practitioners make informed, patient-centered diagnostic decisions.Learning Objectives: Recognize the clinical scenarios where 3D imaging adds value to clinical diagnosis and decision making.Become familiar with radiographic signs of non-odontogenic bone loss and their differential diagnosis.Be able to understand and communicate the radiation dose related to CBCT imaging and its comparison with daily background radiation.
instructors: Setarah Lavasani, DDS, MS, FICD, FGDIA

title: Growing Your Practice Through Endodontics: The Magic Wand
description: Endodontics is the fastest way to grow a restorative practice whether you are a new dentist or seasoned dentist. Next to “crown and bridge,” it can be the second most productive part of the restorative practice. In this session, John teaches you the endodontic essentials that will make you a better dentist by understanding interdisciplinary endodontics and the treasure that is right in front of your eyes. This 90-minute in-depth endodontic education will help you turn your practice into abundance vs. any sense of scarcity that may be creeping into your thoughts.Learning Objectives: Learn the POWER of “The Call List.”Learn the VALUE of "The Pre-Care Call List.”Take “The Tour.”Discover the essential elements of successfully practicing endodontics BEYOND your next level skills.

title: Invisalign Integration & Team Training Part 4: Troubleshooting 101
description: This is an all-day, four-part course (Parts 1-4). Part 4: Troubleshooting 101.We will cover the most common reasons for a tracking issue how to correct them. We will also review the reasons for posterior open bites along with show how to correct them. Everything will make sense after this full-day course and you will walk away feeling clinically confident.Learning Objectives: Understand the top reasons for tracking issues in Invisalign treatment.Learn why posterior open bites can happen and how to resolve them.Utilize treatment planning techniques to avoid from ever getting tracking issues.

title: Modern Periodontal Plastic Surgery: Evidence-Based Approaches to Soft & Hard Tissue Management
description: This lecture will address most common mucogingival problems and periodontal procedures used to address hard and soft tissue defects around natural teeth include gingival augmentation, frenectomy, crown lengthening, and ridge augmentation. The format will be lecture and illustrated via case presentations. These principles will also be presented with respect to dental implant applications.Learning Objectives: Learn various periodontal conditions affecting natural teeth include mucogingival deformities such as recession, inadequate keratinized gingiva, aberrant frenum. Learn and discuss concepts and indications for corrective soft tissue surgical procedures including gingival grafting, root coverage and frenectomy. Learn indications and principles of corrective hard tissue surgical procedures including clinical crown lengthening and post-extraction ridge augmentation techniques.

title: No Fluoride No Problem: Discover the Alternatives
description: The increasing trend of patients declining fluoride treatments or seeking fluoride-free alternatives presents a unique challenge for oral health care professionals. This course is designed to equip practitioners with the expertise to address these concerns and adapt to evolving patient preferences while maintaining the highest standards of care.Fluoride has been a cornerstone of dentistry since its adoption in the 20th century, widely recognized for its effectiveness in preventing caries and strengthening enamel. Various fluoride formulations—including sodium fluoride, stannous fluoride, and acidulated phosphate fluoride—have played integral roles in reducing demineralization and improving oral health outcomes.However, growing concerns around potential risks, such as toxicity and dental fluorosis, have led to a rising demand for viable alternatives.This course offers an in-depth exploration of cutting-edge fluoride-free solutions, including nano-hydroxyapatite, arginine, and other innovative options. Participants will gain insights into the science and clinical applications of these alternatives, enabling them to deliver evidence-based, personalized oral care that aligns with patient needs and preferences.Learning Objectives: Discuss the history and significance of fluoride in dentistry, including its role in caries prevention and enamel remineralization.Identify the various types of fluoride used in dentistry and determine their appropriate clinical applications based on specific oral health needs.Address patient concerns regarding fluoride, including toxicity, dental fluorosis, and the motivations behind the rising demand for fluoride-free alternatives.Evaluate innovative fluoride-free solutions such as nano-hydroxyapatite, arginine, and other emerging materials, focusing on their benefits and clinical applications.Implement evidence-based, personalized oral care plans that meet patient preferences while maintaining high standards of efficacy and safety.
instructors: Joy Void-Holmes, DHS, BSDH, RDH, FADHA, AADH

title: Obstructive Sleep Apnea: Looking Beyond the Teeth & Saving Lives! - Part 2
description: Dentists are uniquely positioned among healthcare providers to easily evaluate for signs of obstructive sleep apnea. Oral appliance therapy performed by a qualified dentist can also be an effective treatment, literally saving lives. In this lecture Dr. Spencer will review the basics of normal sleep, snoring and obstructive sleep apnea in adults and children. Your attendees will most likely be thinking that THEY and everyone they know must have sleep apnea before he is through! Don’t worry though, he will also cover diagnosis and treatment of obstructive sleep apnea, including the dentist’s role and appliance selection.This lecture includes the basics of normal sleep, snoring and sleep apnea in adults in children, and the dental professional's role in screening, referral and treatment of snoring and sleep apnea using oral appliances alone and in conjunction with CPAP as part of a multi-disciplinary team. The bruxism/sleep apnea connection will also be discussed, helping dentists see the relationship between so called "dental parafunction" and sleep apnea. Unlike many manufacturer based lectures, Dr. Spencer will review many of the most popular oral appliances and discuss pros and cons of each.Learning Objectives: Understand normal sleep and how to improve sleep.Understand how obstructive sleep apnea affects adults and children.Become familiar with the dental aspects of screening for and treating OSA.Understand possible problems and side effects of oral appliance therapy.

title: Offensive Dentistry - Part 2
description: Let’s face it, sitting through a lecture about dental caries or caries risk assessment isn’t appealing (in fact it sounds downright boring). However, the science of clinical cariology is beginning to have an impact on restorative techniques and technology. Streptococcus mutans has more to fear than a rheostat, now that we’re armed with salivary diagnostics and bioactive materials.Learning Objectives: Intervene medically in the caries process.Control patient caries risk with focused therapeutics.Design restorations to minimize recurrent decay.Improve the prognosis of restorative treatment.

title: Oral Cavity Cancer Reconstruction from A to Z (Repeat)
description: This presentation will discuss the surgical management of oral cavity cancers and reconstructive methods. It will highlight the evolution of healing following oral cavity cancer treatment.Learning Objectives: Review oral cavity cancer ablative surgical procedures. Discuss relevant oral cavity cancer reconstructive methods.Illustrate the evolution of reconstructed wounds throughout the head and neck cancer treatment journey.

title: Pediatric Sleep Disordered Breathing
description: This session will provide an in-depth look at the clinical features and risk factors associated with Pediatric Sleep-Disordered Breathing (SDB). Attendees will learn about current diagnostic approaches and assessment tools to aid in early identification and intervention. The presentation will also highlight evidence-based management and treatment strategies, emphasizing an interdisciplinary approach to improving sleep, breathing, and overall health outcomes in children.Learning Objectives: Identify the clinical features and risk factors of Pediatric Sleep Disordered Breathing (SDB).Understand the diagnostic approaches and tools for Pediatric Sleep Disordered Breathing.Explore management and treatment strategies for Pediatric Sleep Disordered Breathing.

title: Revolutionizing Dentistry with Artificial Intelligence
description: Artificial intelligence is transforming dentistry and elevating clinical decision support to improve oral health. Discover its applications for detecting caries, calculus, periapical radiolucency (PARL), bone loss, pediatric tooth decay and more indicators of disease. Understand how AI analyzes large amounts of data in seconds to provide dentists with quantified data to support diagnoses and promote operational efficiency. Learn how the color-coded findings improve doctor-patient communication, enhance case presentation, and increase treatment acceptance, leading to better patient outcomes. Learning Objectives: Understand how AI works, its capabilities, and its limitationsWho is using AI? How and Why would You? Learn how AI provides evidence supported documentation that can increase insurance claim approvals Understand how insurance companies are using artificial intelligence too. Review case studies to see how AI enhances clinical decision-making, elevates clinical consistency, and reduces decision fatigueDiscover what’s coming next as dentists harness big data and predictive analytics to provide better care

title: Surviving Back & Neck Pain in Modern Dentistry: A User’s Guide for the Rest of Your Career! - Part 2
description: Is your career hazardous to your physical and mental health? Perhaps killing you slowly? Dentistry has come a long way in the last 150 years. The evolution of anesthesia, dental techniques, materials, equipment, and approaches has evolved yet daily discomfort continues to be tolerated day in and day out by many dental practitioners. Pain appears to be an accepted part of the dental profession, and it seems to be worsening. The patients are pain free but how about the dental staff?Is it possible for the modern day dental professional to counteract the ill-effects of their work on their bodies? Can injecting a dose of regular exercise and dynamic sitting into the operatory facilitate improved workflow with less pain? Is there a conservative approach to consider for pain relief prior to signing up for surgery? Perhaps Mechanical Diagnosis & Therapy is the answer for you.Are you someone who has a nagging pain in your neck or your back that no longer goes away? There is some numbness and tingling in your foot or your dominant hand is a bit clumsier than it used to be. Your sleep is disturbed, and you wake up with a headache. Advil and Motrin no longer “take care of it.” What are you going to do? Before going to the orthopedic/neurosurgeon for surgical intervention; before chronic becomes part of your vocabulary; before filing that disability claim, come and give this a listen!Learning Objectives: Discuss the incidence and prevalence of cervical/lumbar pain in the dental profession.Discuss pain generators in the cervical/lumbar regions.Discuss the unique musculoskeletal needs of the dental profession and the effects of prolonged static loading.Discuss conservative treatment options for cervical/lumbar pain.Discuss Mechanical Diagnosis & Therapy (McKenzie Method) to treatment of referred/radicular back and neck pain as an alternative to or screening prior to surgical intervention.Discuss chair side preventative postures, exercises and strategies.When to seek medical care.
instructors: Timothy Caruso, PT, MBA, MS, CERT.MDT, CEAS

title: The Great Tooth Killer: Cracked Teeth Prevention, Diagnosis & Treatment - Part 2
description: Currently the diagnosis and treatment of cracked and fractured teeth is largely symptom based and is therefore often a sad, end stage diagnosis. The fracturing process is one of dentistry’s last great mysteries and is poorly understood throughout the generalist and specialist dental community. In this engaging and entertaining lecture, you will learn to understand and diagnose pathologic cracks utilizing magnification and other modalities, and treat long before symptoms arise or the tooth becomes unsalvageable. Dr. Clark will teach routine identification of early cracks and teach the Clark Class II saucer shaped nonretentive preparation and Fissurotomy Class I that are a significant departure from the G.V. Black preparations that are a leading cause of cracked teeth. He will share his published approach to diagnosis based on 16X magnification, step by step predictable treatment, endodontic and periodontal implications, and when to extract.Learning Objectives: Have confidence identifying early tooth fracturing based on high level magnification.Learn why new Class II cavity preparations and other modalities are crucial to prevent eventual tooth fracture.Receive a blueprint for diagnosis and treatment of complete and incomplete fractures.Understand the Fissurotomy, Calla Lilly and Overlay preparations for Class I restoration.

title: Time Management & Organization - Part 2
description: Discover how to optimize daily operations and establish effective workflows in a dental practice. This course focuses on enhancing organizational skills, prioritizing tasks, and leveraging tools and technology to boost productivity.Learning Objectives: Recognize typical time-wasting activities and apply strategies to reduce them. Utilize organizational tools and methods to keep your workspace tidy. Organize and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines and alleviate workplace stress.

title: Tracking the CDT Procedure Codes Related to Prevention (And We Don't Mean "Cleanings"!)
description: Dental hygienists are the recognized providers of preventive oral health care. This presentation reviews the assessments which can be performed during a recare appointment, such as extraoral and intraoral cancer screenings, tests and examinations, imaging, and more which help to support comprehensive treatment planning. It also focuses on the CDT procedure codes associated with these preventive services and how to document them effectively.In addition, the course introduces ICD-10 diagnostic codes, which are starting to be required by insurance carriers (Box 34 and 34a on claim form) to demonstrate the medical necessity of treatments and products. Understanding these codes is essential for accurate claim submissions and receiving appropriate reimbursements.Learning Objectives: Describe the CDT procedure codes associated with preventive services including; Pre-diagnostic services, Diagnostic imaging, Tests and examination, other preventive services and products.Describe how to document preventive products that support caries management and hypersensitivity.Provide a foundational understanding of the ICD-10 diagnostic codes and their application in documenting medical necessity for preventive services and products.
instructors: Kathy S. Forbes, RDH, BS, FADHA

title: Understanding Autism: A Dual Perspective
description:
Autism Spectrum Disorder can present a variety of challenges and needs in both the dental office and home setting. As a dentist, providing care for individuals with ASD requires a comprehensive understanding of the condition’s characteristics and how these might affect the dental experience. As a parent of a child with ASD, navigating dental care can sometimes be daunting, but it’s an essential part of ensuring the child’s overall health and well-being. Supporting individuals with ASD during dental visits requires sensitivity, preparation, and understanding from both dentists and parents. By working together, we can ensure that dental care is as comfortable and effective as possible.Learning Objectives: Discuss Autism Spectrum Disorder and its common characteristics.Review dental challenges from both the parent and dentist perspective.
.Discuss best treatment options and collaborative care approaches.

title: Utilizing 3D Printing Technology in General Dentistry (Repeat)
description: The opportunities for 3D printing and scanning and integration into other dental office systems like Cone Beam CT imaging is expanding at an alarming rate. This course will discuss the practical ways to incorporate this technology into the general dental office and the tremendous potential that it has for Return on Investment (ROI). Topics will include 3D printed study models, surgical guides, in-office aligners and occlusal guards.
