description: Patients enter our practices every day that are at various stages of edentulism, however, knowing when to make the jump to an All-on-X can be challenging. In this lecture, we will review treatment options and discuss key principles needed for success. Additionally, conversion techniques will be reviewed and clinical steps to fabricate the definitive prosthesis. Following the completion of this lecture, you should have improved confidence in the treatment of the All-on-X patient.Learning Objectives: Learn to treatment plan the All-on-X patient.Discuss digital workflows for immediate denture conversions.Review digital workflows involved with fabricating the final restoration.

title: Appliance Therapy in Everyday Dentistry - Part 2
description: Various types of appliances, applications of each appliance, and principles of appliance therapy in joints, muscles, and teeth. Utilizing bite splint therapy and understanding various appliance designs can be an invaluable advantage in a more comprehensive approach to treatment planning. A step by step overview of the different appliances will be discussed and simplified for applications in everyday dentistry. Learn the indications, contraindications, and benefits to the different appliances with applications in dental cases and applied to predictable dental outcomes.Learning Objectives: Review of TMJ and muscle examination and anatomy.Review of Occlusal Muscle Disharmony.Focus on Principles and Design of Bite Splint therapy.Discussion of all various appliance designs and advantages and disadvantages to appliance selection.Focus on Principles and Design of Bite Splint therapy.Discussion of all various appliance designs and advantages and disadvantages to appliance selection.Discussion of integration of appliance therapy, principles of occlusion, and equilibration.How to communicate and collaborate with your team and patients with Appliance Therapy.

title: Dealing with Complications in Implant Dentistry: A Case Journey - Part 2
description: Complications in dentistry are never a good thing! If the greatest crime the devil ever created was making you think that there are no complications in implant dentistry, we have a lot to talk about. This presentation is all about complications and there are many of them to discuss and how you can avoid them. Shooting yourself in the foot is a bad thing, it's even worse when you reload the gun. So let's share our experiences in a journey.Learning Objectives: 4 different types of complications.5 ways to avoid complications.3 examples of cases which failed and how to avoid that!
instructors: Waji Khan, BSc, DDS, MBA, MEd

title: Oral Cavity Cancer Reconstruction from A to Z (Repeat)
description: This presentation will discuss the surgical management of oral cavity cancers and reconstructive methods. It will highlight the evolution of healing following oral cavity cancer treatment.Learning Objectives: Review oral cavity cancer ablative surgical procedures. Discuss relevant oral cavity cancer reconstructive methods.Illustrate the evolution of reconstructed wounds throughout the head and neck cancer treatment journey.

title: Upholding Dental Ethics in the Age of AI
description: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into oral healthcare presents a new frontier of ethical challenges that extend from dental school admissions to the clinical chair. As these powerful tools become embedded in diagnostics, practice management, and research, clinicians must proactively address complex questions. This session moves beyond broad societal concerns to examine specific ethical dilemmas in the oral health context. Topics will include algorithmic bias in diagnostic software, patient autonomy in AI-assisted treatment decisions, data privacy in machine learning models, and maintaining professional accountability when relying on automated systems. Using frameworks established by the American Dental Association and the American Medical Association, this presentation offers a structured approach for evaluating and navigating the ethical landscape of AI in order to uphold the highest standard of patient care.Learning Objectives: Identify ethical quandaries when utilizing AI in oral health.Apply current ethical frameworks as established by the American Dental Association.Evaluate the ethical implications of AI on professional integrity.

In this interactive, fast-moving course, Joshua shares the current trends in dental materials and technologies. Learn how materials have changed over the past few years and how those changes can affect your procedures and practice.This session will focus on direct restorative dentistry and bonding.Learning Objectives: Illuminate growing trends and discover the best new products and innovations on the market.Explore the impact new products/technologies have on the practice’s bottom line.Explore the three etching techniques and learn when each are appropriate.Analyze how bulk fill posterior composites compare to their traditional counterparts.Discuss time saving placement techniques for bulk fill posterior composites.Review universal adhesives and their advantages.Discuss when luting is an appropriate choice instead of bonding.Explore many other products and items that can make your life easier and your dentistry faster.

instructors: Thomas Viola, RPh, CCP, CDE, CPMP

title: Beyond the Hype: Practical AI for Oral Heath
description: Since 2022, generative artificial intelligence (AI) has rapidly transformed the technological landscape, becoming a focal point of innovation across industries. As AI becomes increasingly integrated into oral healthcare, providers must develop an understanding of its fundamentals, capabilities, and limitations. This session will explore the evolution of AI, its applications in oral healthcare and beyond, and immediate, practical use cases for private practice.Learning Objectives: Define AI and AI adjacent terms.Analyze current AI use in oral healthcare.Identify opportunities to further integrate AI into oral healthcare.

title: Digital & CAD/CAM Biomimetic Dentistry - Part 1
description: This course introduces biomimetic dentistry, focusing on the advantages of indirect biomimetic restorations. Participants will explore how these restorations outperform traditional ceramics by closely replicating the natural tooth structure. The course covers the biomimetic workflow and demonstrates how integrating digital and CAD/CAM technology enhances precision, efficiency, and quality. Key insights into equipment selection, design optimization, and material choices will be provided, along with case presentations showcasing the potential of in-office production.Learning Objectives: Understand the principles of biomimetic dentistry and their relevance to indirect restorations.Identify key differences between biomimetic and traditional indirect ceramic restorations.Learn the steps involved in the biomimetic indirect workflow, from planning to execution.Explore how CAD/CAM technology enhances the precision and efficiency of biomimetic restorations.Choose appropriate equipment, materials, and techniques for achieving high-quality restorations.

title: Getting Them to Ask for Treatment: A Periodontal Case Acceptance Program - Part 1
description: We see patients in a perceived state of health. Despite the unending research around the prevalence of oral disease, dental providers are met with a myriad of challenges when it comes to optimized case acceptance for advanced dental treatment.These challenges are often barriers to upholding fundamental educational principles, connecting patients to their value system and dental IQ, to name a few. The complication: these challenges are getting in the way of patients receiving the optimal treatment and level of care they need, it is affecting the metrics of the dental practice, and most concerning: oral disease prevalence continues to soar.Using the “Assessment – Strategy – Knowledge” technique, learn how you can achieve high level treatment case acceptance simply by changing the dialogue around dentistry and ultimately: getting them to ask you for their needed treatment! Learn about how the ASSESSMENT phase provides critical insights to develop a STRATEGY to support patients in empowering themselves to approach dental communication from a space of KNOWLEDGE aimed to optimize their complex care.Learning Objectives: Understand how the ASSESSMENT phase is a powerful educational tool for dental patients.Develop a customized STRATEGY to foster patient-centered, value-driven conversations.Learn to integrate KNOWLEDGE throughout the dental hygiene process to enhance patient awareness of oral and systemic health.Collaborate in a workshop setting to apply diverse communication styles in patient care.
instructors: Katrina M. Sanders, RDH, BSDH, M.Ed, RF

title: Myofunctional Appliance Therapy & Orthodontic Force Systems for the Growing Patient
description: Oral and facial muscle tone and posture plays a crucial roll in proper development of face. Treating growing children in the mixed dentition presents challenges and opportunities. As orthodontics increases considerations in diagnosis to include aberrant respiration and function, so too should we consider therapies aimed at addressing these etiologies. Myofunctional appliance therapy aims to correct malocclusions by first addressing underlying breathing and swallowing disorders. When combined with traditional orthodontics and orthopedics, myofunctional appliance therapy can enhance treatment outcomes, shorten treatment times, and improve stability.Learning Objectives: Understand environmental factors that contribute to growth and development of the face, alveolus, and dentition.Recognize potential causes of malocclusion in young patients.Understand how early intervention can intercept developing malocclusions.

title: Oral Surgery for the General Practitioner: How to Make it Flow Better in Your Clinic - Part 1
description: This presentation is to discuss and demonstrate oral surgical procedures that can be performed by the GP safely in office with ease and confidence. I will discuss my own journey in this discipline and go over tips, tricks and mentorship I have gained in my 20+ year career as a wet-fingered GP. We will be using a combination of cases with photos, videos, CBCT, radiographs and history and physical exam data to learn how to extract teeth, raise flaps, place implants and grafts, suture well, draw blood for PRF, interpret blood tests, perform biopsies, expose teeth for orthodontics and do frenectomies.Learning Objectives: How to extract teeth, both erupted and impacted with confidence.How to venipuncture a patient to draw blood to make PRF in your office.How to use blood tests to augment the surgical outcomes for your patients.How to perform various surgical procedures in office like frenectomies, biopsy and ortho exposures.How to deal with complications and post-op infections and manage your surgical patients.
instructors: Waji Khan, BSc, DDS, MBA, MEd

title: Revolutionizing Dentistry with Artificial Intelligence
description: Artificial intelligence is transforming dentistry and elevating clinical decision support to improve oral health. Discover its applications for detecting caries, calculus, periapical radiolucency (PARL), bone loss, pediatric tooth decay and more indicators of disease. Understand how AI analyzes large amounts of data in seconds to provide dentists with quantified data to support diagnoses and promote operational efficiency. Learn how the color-coded findings improve doctor-patient communication, enhance case presentation, and increase treatment acceptance, leading to better patient outcomes. Learning Objectives: Understand how AI works, its capabilities, and its limitationsWho is using AI? How and Why would You? Learn how AI provides evidence supported documentation that can increase insurance claim approvals Understand how insurance companies are using artificial intelligence too. Review case studies to see how AI enhances clinical decision-making, elevates clinical consistency, and reduces decision fatigueDiscover what’s coming next as dentists harness big data and predictive analytics to provide better care

title: "Hurts So...Swell!" Dental Pain Management For the Entire Dental Team - Part 1
description: Qualifies for MATE requirement hours.Management of acute odontogenic pain is accomplished through a clinical approach that successfully incorporates both opioid and non-opioid analgesics. However, moderate to severe dental pain often occurs well after office hours and away from our immediate supervision. Thus, it is critical for clinicians to be able to accurately identify a patient's need for pain control, develop an individualized pain management plan and assess the efficacy of analgesic agents employed.This program will provide strategies for effectively managing dental pain with opioid and non-opioid analgesics and analgesic adjuvants, while also mitigating associated risks. Special emphasis will be given to appropriate practices for prescribing analgesics as well as identification and treatment of opioid analgesic abuse.Learning Objectives: Understand the pharmacology and mechanism of action of opioid and non-opioid analgesics, as well as their potential for abuse.Discuss best practices for prescribing opioid analgesics as part of an individualized patient pain management plan.Describe techniques useful in recognizing and identifying potential addiction as well as avoiding potential drug diversion.Learn current protocols for the management and treatment of opioid addiction.Examine guidelines for proper storage and disposal of unused dosages.
instructors: Thomas Viola, RPh, CCP, CDE, CPMP

title: The Complete Face: Botox Live-Patient Training Certification Course ($1,600)
description: If you're looking for a Botox course that actually teaches the whole face—not just a fraction of it—The Complete Face is that course. This program is the only training that combines upper and lower facial cosmetic treatment and therapeutic Botox for TMJ in one comprehensive seminar. No multiple “levels.” No return trips to learn the rest. One course. Thorough and practical.This is anatomy-first, evidence-based education—not a commercial demonstration course. You’ll learn to evaluate, plan, dose, and inject with clarity and intention so you can immediately offer meaningful, safe, and high-value tox treatments in your practice.To enroll, candidates must supply:Valid dental licenseMalpractice insurance declarations page1. Dentists provide their own patient models for the afternoon hands-on session. 2. All dentists who wish to inject patients at the event must demonstrate proof of holding a valid Washington State dental license or dental license in another state (Under state law -RCW 18.32.030, dentists from out-of-state are allowed to participate in continuing education events sanctioned by WSDA ) 3. All dentists must provide proof of current malpractice insurance in the form of a declarations page.Additional Supplies to bring: camera (not phone), loupes or readers (optional), gloves if allergic to latex.Learning Objectives: Identify specific areas of the aging face and features that can be improved with tox treatment.Discuss indications for therapeutic treatment with botulinum toxins in the head and neck.Conduct a thorough patient consultation to evaluate the potential need for and assess the suitability of facial injectables.Avoid, recognize and manage adverse events.Demonstrate accurate photo-documentation and written documentation.

title: Digital & CAD/CAM Biomimetic Dentistry - Part 2
description: This course introduces biomimetic dentistry, focusing on the advantages of indirect biomimetic restorations. Participants will explore how these restorations outperform traditional ceramics by closely replicating the natural tooth structure. The course covers the biomimetic workflow and demonstrates how integrating digital and CAD/CAM technology enhances precision, efficiency, and quality. Key insights into equipment selection, design optimization, and material choices will be provided, along with case presentations showcasing the potential of in-office production.Learning Objectives: Understand the principles of biomimetic dentistry and their relevance to indirect restorations.Identify key differences between biomimetic and traditional indirect ceramic restorations.Learn the steps involved in the biomimetic indirect workflow, from planning to execution.Explore how CAD/CAM technology enhances the precision and efficiency of biomimetic restorations.Choose appropriate equipment, materials, and techniques for achieving high-quality restorations.

title: From Classroom to Clinic: How AI is Reshaping Oral Health Education
description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been widely regarded as one of the most significant modern educational disruptors. While public attention has focused on generative AI, other powerful tools like computer vision and machine learning are fundamentally changing clinical education. This session will explore a dental school’s experience using an AI-powered clinical decision support (CDS) tool in a comprehensive clinical care model to improve the identification of common oral pathologies, optimize the instructional experience, and impact educational outcomes. Attendees will gain insights into the practical application of these tools, potential challenges, and benefits, ultimately aiming to elevate the quality of clinical education and patient care.Learning Objectives: Describe the fundamentals of artificial intelligence and its relevance to oral health education.Explore the capabilities of clinical decision support tools in an educational setting.Apply strategies for leveraging AI technologies to enhance student learning and clinical outcomes.

title: Getting Them to Ask for Treatment: A Periodontal Case Acceptance Program - Part 2
description: We see patients in a perceived state of health. Despite the unending research around the prevalence of oral disease, dental providers are met with a myriad of challenges when it comes to optimized case acceptance for advanced dental treatment.These challenges are often barriers to upholding fundamental educational principles, connecting patients to their value system and dental IQ, to name a few. The complication: these challenges are getting in the way of patients receiving the optimal treatment and level of care they need, it is affecting the metrics of the dental practice, and most concerning: oral disease prevalence continues to soar.Using the “Assessment – Strategy – Knowledge” technique, learn how you can achieve high level treatment case acceptance simply by changing the dialogue around dentistry and ultimately: getting them to ask you for their needed treatment! Learn about how the ASSESSMENT phase provides critical insights to develop a STRATEGY to support patients in empowering themselves to approach dental communication from a space of KNOWLEDGE aimed to optimize their complex care.Learning Objectives: Understand how the ASSESSMENT phase is a powerful educational tool for dental patients.Develop a customized STRATEGY to foster patient-centered, value-driven conversations.Learn to integrate KNOWLEDGE throughout the dental hygiene process to enhance patient awareness of oral and systemic health.Collaborate in a workshop setting to apply diverse communication styles in patient care.
instructors: Katrina M. Sanders, RDH, BSDH, M.Ed, RF

title: Immediate Dental Implants
description: This engaging lecture will explore the modern practice of immediate dental implants, a transformative approach in restorative dentistry that enhances patient outcomes and satisfaction. Attendees will gain insights into the latest techniques and materials that facilitate immediate placement of dental implants on the same day as tooth extraction. We will discuss the biological principles underlying immediate implants, including osseointegration and the critical factors influencing success rates. The lecture will cover patient selection criteria, emphasizing the importance of careful assessment of bone quality, quantity, and overall health. Case studies will illustrate real-world applications, highlighting both successes and challenges faced in clinical settings. Participants will learn about advanced imaging technologies, such as CBCT or intraoral scanning, that aid in treatment planning and improve predictability. Additionally, we will explore the latest innovations in implant design and surface technology that promote faster healing and stability. The session will also address common complications and management strategies, equipping practitioners with the knowledge to navigate potential pitfalls in immediate implant placement. Join us to expand your understanding of immediate dental implants and enhance your practice with evidence-based strategies that can lead to improved patient care and streamlined workflows.Learning Objectives: Describe the principles and benefits of immediate dental implants for our patients and practices.Identify ideal candidates for immediate dental implants by understanding their indications and contraindications.Understand the techniques and materials that facilitate immediate implant placement.

title: Oral Surgery for the General Practitioner: How to Make it Flow Better in Your Clinic - Part 2
description: This presentation is to discuss and demonstrate oral surgical procedures that can be performed by the GP safely in office with ease and confidence. I will discuss my own journey in this discipline and go over tips, tricks and mentorship I have gained in my 20+ year career as a wet-fingered GP. We will be using a combination of cases with photos, videos, CBCT, radiographs and history and physical exam data to learn how to extract teeth, raise flaps, place implants and grafts, suture well, draw blood for PRF, interpret blood tests, perform biopsies, expose teeth for orthodontics and do frenectomies.Learning Objectives: How to extract teeth, both erupted and impacted with confidence.How to venipuncture a patient to draw blood to make PRF in your office.How to use blood tests to augment the surgical outcomes for your patients.How to perform various surgical procedures in office like frenectomies, biopsy and ortho exposures.How to deal with complications and post-op infections and manage your surgical patients.
instructors: Waji Khan, BSc, DDS, MBA, MEd

title: "Hurts So...Swell!" Dental Pain Management For the Entire Dental Team - Part 2
description: Qualifies for MATE requirement hours.Management of acute odontogenic pain is accomplished through a clinical approach that successfully incorporates both opioid and non-opioid analgesics. However, moderate to severe dental pain often occurs well after office hours and away from our immediate supervision. Thus, it is critical for clinicians to be able to accurately identify a patient's need for pain control, develop an individualized pain management plan and assess the efficacy of analgesic agents employed.This program will provide strategies for effectively managing dental pain with opioid and non-opioid analgesics and analgesic adjuvants, while also mitigating associated risks. Special emphasis will be given to appropriate practices for prescribing analgesics as well as identification and treatment of opioid analgesic abuse.Learning Objectives: Understand the pharmacology and mechanism of action of opioid and non-opioid analgesics, as well as their potential for abuse.Discuss best practices for prescribing opioid analgesics as part of an individualized patient pain management plan.Describe techniques useful in recognizing and identifying potential addiction as well as avoiding potential drug diversion.Learn current protocols for the management and treatment of opioid addiction.Examine guidelines for proper storage and disposal of unused dosages.
instructors: Thomas Viola, RPh, CCP, CDE, CPMP

title: "If It Wasn't Documented, It Wasn't Done" - The Role of Documentation in Claims Prevention
description:
In dental malpractice litigation, documentation is oftentimes the single most decisive factor in determining whether a case can be successfully defended. Even clinically appropriate care can become indefensible when records are incomplete, inconsistent, or omit key details. This course provides a comprehensive exploration of the documentation practices that protect clinicians - and the documentation failures that routinely undermine defense efforts. Participants will examine real-world case studies to understand how documentation errors, omissions, ambiguous phrasing, and alterations can magnify liability. Attendees will also gain insight into how Plaintiff attorneys use documentation gaps to build their cases-and how well-built records can dismantle those arguments. This presentation will be provided by Jacqueline Clarke, J.D., a litigation attorney, who currently serves as MedPro's Lead Dental Risk Consultant. MedPro insureds who attend this presentation may be eligible to earn a 5% risk management premium credit* for the next policy renewal. *Approval by MedPro does not imply acceptance by a state or provincial board. Premium credit eligibility and amount subject to state insurance filings and policy type. Completion of a risk management course does not imply or guarantee renewal.Learning Objectives: Identify the trends in dental malpractice litigation, including the most common types of procedures, and injuries, which lead to malpractice lawsuits.Recognize what constitutes the clinical chart, and how adequacy, content, and mechanics, all contribute to successful (or unsuccessful) documentation
Assess the documentation pitfalls most likely to lead to malpractice exposure or compromise a defense. Document diagnostic rationale, informed consent, and patient communication in a defensible, patient-centered manner. Understand how courts and attorneys interpret dental records, including the weight given to omissions and inaccuracies.

title: ABC (Alignment, Brightening, Composite Bonding): Non-Invasive Smile Enhancements
description: The ABC protocol—Alignment, Brightening, Composite Bonding—offers a sustainable, minimally invasive approach to preserve natural tooth structure while achieving aesthetic and functional excellence. Alignment enhances the bite and creates restorative space. Brightening unifies and elevates tooth shades. Composite bonding perfects form and function without removing healthy enamel, offering protection and reducing sensitivity. Advanced composites like Tokuyama OMNICHROMA provide the finishing touch for lifelike, durable smiles, seamlessly resolving black triangles and diastemas.Learning Objectives: Learn the steps for an effective smile consultation and comprehensive exam.Understand how alignment treatments improve bite and create restorative space for greater durability.Understand how brightening creates an enhanced shade for bonding and maintains teeth brightness.Understand how composite bonding enhances aesthetics, reduces sensitivity, and preserves tooth structure.Learn how to use Tokuyama OMNICHROMA, the chameleon composite, in the OmniGap Protocol to simply and predictably close black triangles and diastemas.Learn the principles of retention, maintenance, and longevity for lasting results.

title: AI in Practice: Navigating the Legal & Technical Maze
description: As Artificial Intelligence (AI) moves from theory into operatories, clinicians face a critical challenge: how to adopt powerful new tools responsibly and safely. While AI promises to enhance diagnostics and streamline operations, it also introduces new questions about data security, diagnostic liability, and regulatory compliance. This session provides a practical framework for navigating this landscape and help establish clear principles for vetting and implementing AI technology, covering everything from vendor due diligence and HIPAA-compliant data handling to effective staff training. Attendees will learn how to integrate these tools to improve patient care without compromising security or complicating clinical workflows.Learning Objectives: Describe legal obligations for technology in healthcare.Create strategies for safe technology adoption.Apply strategies to AI tools.

title: Dealing with Complications in Implant Dentistry: A Case Journey - Part 1
description: Complications in dentistry are never a good thing! If the greatest crime the devil ever created was you thinking that there are no complications in dentistry, we have a lot to talk about. This presentation is all about complications and there are many of them to discuss and how you can avoid them. Shooting yourself in the foot is a bad thing, it's even worse when you reload the gun. So let's share our experiences in a journey.Learning Objectives: 4 different types of complications.5 ways to avoid complications.3 examples of cases which failed and how to avoid that!
instructors: Waji Khan, BSc, DDS, MBA, MEd

title: Digital Integration in Esthetic Dentistry & Full-Mouth Rehabilitations
description: This course delves into the integration of digital technologies in both esthetic dentistry and full-mouth rehabilitations. Participants will explore how digital tools, such as smile design software and 3D printing, streamline treatment planning and enhance communication with patients and labs. Whether for simple esthetic cases or complex rehabilitations, this course demonstrates how digital techniques can improve efficiency, precision, and predictability. By combining advanced digital solutions with traditional methods like mock-ups, practitioners will be equipped to deliver superior outcomes across a wide spectrum of cases.Learning Objectives: Understand the benefits and limitations of digital techniques for esthetic treatments and full-mouth rehabilitations.Utilize digital tools and software to design and plan both simple esthetic cases and complex rehabilitations.Incorporate 3D printing and mock-ups into workflows to enhance efficiency and precision in treatment execution.Improve communication and predictability through the use of digital methods across various case complexities.Apply digital solutions to optimize treatment planning and lab collaboration for esthetic and rehabilitation cases.

title: Launch & Lead: New-Era Dental Startup Strategies to Accelerate Success
description: A modern blueprint to build — or rebuild — the dental practice of your dreams. Every dentist wants the same thing: meaning in their work, freedom in their life, and a practice they are proud to lead. Yet many feel stuck — overwhelmed by insurance pressures, slowed by outdated systems, or uninspired by the associate treadmill.What if the path to a fulfilling and profitable dental career isn’t harder work… but intentional design? What if launching a practice — or re-launching your current one — could actually be simpler than buying into an old model or grinding as an associate?Dr. Chaz Vittitow did exactly that. In one of the most competitive markets in Washington, he launched a fee-for-service startup that collected over $1.2M in year one, became the highest-reviewed office in the county, and still took 8 weeks off — all without selling out to corporate dentistry or burning out.In this transformative session, you'll learn:✅ A step-by-step plan to launch a startup with intention, clarity, and confidence✅ How to apply startup thinking to an existing practice to refresh culture, systems, and growth✅ Simple, high-impact patient experience and communication strategies✅ Team-building and leadership skills every dentist can master✅ Ethical, relationship-based marketing that attracts ideal patients✅ How to design a practice — and career — aligned with your values, lifestyle, and goalsWho this is for:Dental students mapping their pathNew grads and associates seeking confidence, autonomy, and directionPractice owners ready to reinvent, reignite, or refreshAny dentist craving freedom, fulfillment, and momentumThe promiseYou’ll walk away with clear steps, practical tools, and a proven framework to grow with confidence — whether you’re launching from scratch or reinventing your current practice.This is your chance to build a practice that fuels your life — not the other way around. With the right strategies, the right people, and a compelling vision, there has never been a better

title: Street Drugs, Illicit Drugs & Licit Drugs Used Illicitly: Medical & Dental Considerations - Part 1
description: It’s a fast-paced world and many patients may seek the assistance of substances to cope and get through their busy, trying days. The use and abuse of both licit and illicit substances has the potential to produce adverse reactions and life-threatening medical emergencies during dental treatment. This comprehensive program will explore strategies for identifying the dental patient using and abusing substances, as well as techniques for effective patient management and treatment. Special emphasis will be given to a discussion of patients with previously existing or current substance abuse and the impact on patients using cannabis for medical and recreational purposes.Learning Objectives: Discuss the impact of substance dependence and abuse on dental therapy and on overall patient health.Identify the most common illicit and licit substances of abuse and dependence and discuss: street names, common adverse effects, oral manifestations, dental treatment considerations.Describe techniques useful in identifying and successfully managing patient substance use and dependency.
instructors: Thomas Viola, RPh, CCP, CDE, CPMP

title: Canines: Good Dog, Bad Dog...Plus, Retainers Gone Wild(cat)
description: Canine teeth are often admired, even revered in our profession as cornerstone teeth for occlusion, function and smile esthetics. While they can often be a dentist’s “best friend”, canines can also be quite the troublemakers. As “good dogs”, canines can serve as esthetic, natural tooth replacements for congenitally missing lateral incisors. In the developing dentition, because canine follicles form the furthest from the occlusal plane, there is ample opportunity for them to become “bad dogs”, losing their way along the path to eruption. This can lead to ectopic positioning, impaction, ankylosis or even damage to surrounding teeth. This lecture uses numerous clinical examples from private practice to present a novel approach to manage congenitally missing lateral incisors. We will also explore the diagnosis, etiologies, consequences and treatment solutions for ectopically erupting/impacted canines. Bonus: Maintaining long-term lower anterior tooth alignment is one of the biggest challenges in orthodontics. This has led to the commonplace use of bonded retainers. Certain wires used for fixed retainers can become active later in life, leading to severe displacement of lower incisor roots. These patients are often referred to a periodontist for gingival grafting. This lecture will use clinical examples to demonstrate the role that the orthodontist can play in helping the periodontist resolve these challenging situations.Learning Objectives: Be exposed to an alternative treatment option for the replacement of congenitally missing maxillary lateral incisors.Better understand the diagnosis and treatment options for ectopically erupting canines.Be able to identify the signs of an active fixed lingual retainer and be knowledgeable of the interdisciplinary approach to treat these cases.

title: Upholding Dental Ethics in the Age of AI
description: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into oral healthcare presents a new frontier of ethical challenges that extend from dental school admissions to the clinical chair. As these powerful tools become embedded in diagnostics, practice management, and research, clinicians must proactively address complex questions. This session moves beyond broad societal concerns to examine specific ethical dilemmas in the oral health context. Topics will include algorithmic bias in diagnostic software, patient autonomy in AI-assisted treatment decisions, data privacy in machine learning models, and maintaining professional accountability when relying on automated systems. Using frameworks established by the American Dental Association and the American Medical Association, this presentation offers a structured approach for evaluating and navigating the ethical landscape of AI in order to uphold the highest standard of patient care.Learning Objectives: Identify ethical quandaries when utilizing AI in oral health.Apply current ethical frameworks as established by the American Dental Association and the American Medical Association.Evaluate the ethical implications of AI on professional integrity.

title: Ailing and Failing Implants? Strategies to Ensure Implant Health in the Dental Office
description: Dental implants are an important treatment modality for replacement of missing teeth, with millions of dental implants placed yearly. Patients often erroneously get the impression that "implants are like natural teeth" and will last a lifetime. However,biologic complications (peri-implant disease) may occur and if not properly managed ,may lead to implant failure. Thus the ability for clinicians to recognize peri-implant disease when it occurs,and understand the key strategies for timely management is crucial.

It is designed to build participant confidence in performing CPR skills in a team environment for dental professionals with a duty to respond. This course teaches the steps to perform a rapid assessment, Basic Life Support skills, and rapid defibrillation including the use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED). The BLS curriculum supports the delivery of team-based professional-level resuscitation.

title: The Six Keys to Attachment-Less Clear Aligner Orthodontics
description:
Resin attachments have become very ubiquitous in modern clear aligner orthodontics. It has become "accepted" that they are essential- without much intellectual challenge.
It is our duty as scientists, however- to constantly question the status quo and make sure that "accepted" protocols are actually necessary and beneficial.
Dr. McFarlane has done just that- and the answers may intrigue you.
To support this statement: he will present his "Six Keys to Atttachment-Less Clear Aligner Orthodontics."
requirements:
Audio Visual Additional/Special Requirements: null
Audio Visual Requirements: Yes
Learning Objective #1: Discover why attachments have become so ingrained in our thinking and planning.
Learning Objective #2: Understand that there are effective alternatives.
Learning Objective #3: Discover actionable strategies to minimize attachments in clear aligner orthodontics.
Pre-requisites: null
Session Price: null
Session Sponsor: Co-sponsored by Straumann

title: The Magic of Air for Managing Dysbiotic Biofilms Around Implants and Teeth
description: Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT) is an evidence-based strategic plan for upsetting the underworld of biofilms in the oral cavity. This novel approach to biofilm removal incorporates the magic of air, powder and water to irradicate dysbiotic biofilms clinging to teeth and implants. With Guided Biofilm Therapy the patient and clinician benefit from an easy, gentle and effective method of biofilm removal from implants, exposed root surfaces, delicate restorative materials, and difficult to access periodontal pockets.

title: Revolutionizing Dry Mouth Management
description: Dry mouth is one of the most overlooked, yet debilitating conditions impacting both oral and systemic health. Its consequences extend far beyond discomfort, contributing to oral disease, malnutrition, social isolation, and a significantly reduced quality of life. "Let's Talk Solutions" will provide you with new and innovative strategies to successfully address this underappreciated yet critical aspect of oral health. Our patients deserve our attention to this often underdiagnosed and mismanaged condition.

Learn essential TMJ physical exam steps and conservative "toolkits" for soft tissue head and neck pain. This course features live demonstrations of self-directed physiotherapy protocols, allowing you to diagnose accurately and provide patients with immediate, actionable relief. Move beyond theory into predictable clinical results.

description: This case-based presentation explores pediatric dental scenarios that linger long after the clinic closes; cases that generate uncertainty and stress in clinical practice. Through real-world examples, participants will examine how effective treatment planning depends on integrated clinical reasoning that incorporates medical, developmental, and social determinants of health. Emphasis is placed on evidence-based decision-making to support safe and effective pediatric care.

description: Have you heard those terms before? We will discuss the current evidence based recommendations for treating primary teeth during this period of increased decay and decreased parenting. We’ll look at handpiece use and light cure pulp temperature changes, alternative treatments for pulp therapy and review the options of materials for maximum efficiency and biocompatibility. Pain control is essential for successful treatment and will be reviewed.

description: Let's face it, doctors are not the best communicators. Staff members are often the people tapped to clarify and explain what's going on because they are trusted by the families. This is an evidence based primer on what parents need to hear about the science behind keeping a child caries free. Discussions of diet, frequency and food and the effect on the oral biome are explored. And finally, managing parent and child behavior will be reviewed.

description: Full-arch rehabilitation is a complex, evolving field in implant dentistry. Options such as overdentures, All-on-4, and zygomatic implants support fixed and removable solutions for edentulism. Digital and material advances now enable more efficient, minimally invasive care. This course reviews its evolution, current innovations, and future trends in implant rehabilitation.

title: Orthodontic Interventions: When To Treat and When To Wait
description: In our younger patients, we can see that not everyone grows in a perfect Class I occlusion. In this session we will evaluate a number of different malocclusions and eruption challenges that may present themselves as our growing patients move from their primary to their permanent dentition. We will talk about when it's beneficial to intervene, when it's best to wait, and what treatment recommendations you may hear from the orthodontist to help optimize your patients' growth and development.

description: Technology, AI and advanced digital workflows are reshaping how clinicians diagnose, plan and deliver care. In this forward thinking lecture, double board certified specialist Dr. Dan Berant explores the connection between oral health and overall wellness and the role AI plays in supporting optimal treatment. Through biologic principles, interdisciplinary planning and innovative digital solutions supporting procedures such as MARPE and tooth autotransplantation, patients can receive truly personalized care.

title: Future-Proofing Your Dental Practice: A Marketing Strategy That Wins Tomorrow
description: The way patients discover, research, and choose dental practices is changing faster than ever. Search behavior is shifting, AI is reshaping visibility, and traditional marketing tactics alone are no longer enough to ensure long-term growth. This course is designed to help dental practices future-proof their marketing by understanding where patient acquisition is headed and how to build a strategy that adapts, scales, and sustains success over time. Attendees will learn how to position their practice for continued visibility in an AI-driven search landscape, create trust and authority before a patient ever picks up the phone, and implement marketing systems that evolve alongside technology and consumer behavior. Rather than chasing short-term tactics, this session focuses on building a resilient marketing foundation that protects your practice against platform changes, algorithm updates, and increasing competition.
requirements: Compare how patients find dentists across traditional search engines and emerging AI-powered platforms, and assess positioning strategies that lead to being recommended rather than merely ranked Learn the components of traditional dental marketing strategies and they differ from future-proof strategies, emphasizing AI-driven search visibility, reputation signals, and content authority Recognize how patient discovery is evolving versus traditional discovery models, explaining why SEO and paid adv

title: Smart Simplicity: Leveraging AI to Simplify 2D and 3D Imaging Workflows
description: Discover how artificial intelligence is revolutionizing dental imaging by simplifying complex processes and enhancing clinical outcomes. This course explores practical strategies to integrate AI into everyday workflows, combining diagnostic and workflow automation to eliminate manual steps, reduce learning curves, and boost efficiency. Learn how AI-driven tools expand the clinical applications of CBCT for implant planning, endodontic diagnosis, and interdisciplinary case evaluation, while creating unified imaging workflows across multiple modalities: CBCT, 2D radiography, photography, and intraoral scans. Using real-world case studies, you’ll see how AI automations streamline treatment planning, improve patient experiences, and empower clinicians to deliver more precise, comprehensive care.

title: Endodontic Specialty Group Program
description: Designed by Specialists, Open to All Endodontic Microsurgery Outcome Assessment The probability of success, as evaluated through a critical analysis of the literature, is an important step in practicing evidence-based dentistry. The prognostic data regarding endodontic microsurgery indicate that microsurgical success rates are significantly superior to those of traditional surgery. At the same time, within the surgical technique, each step of the procedure is important to ensure a successful outcome. This presentation will critically review the available literature to evaluate mitigating variables, including the indication for retreatment before surgery, reversal of success over a longer observation period, intraoperative variables such as the use of magnification and illumination, periodontal involvement, root-end management techniques, as well as potential reasons for failure and the expectations from resurgery.
requirements: Appraise the prognostic literature to extract data relevant for clinical practice Critically review the available literature to evaluate mitigating variables, including the indication for retreatment before surgery, reversal of success over a longer observation period, and intraoperative variables Explain the impact of prognostic factors on the outcome of microsurgical techniques * Tuition includes coffee and pastry break with an opportunity to network with peers at 10:00 – 10:30 am. Endorsed by

title: Secrets of Dental Startups and Practice Acquisitions
description: In this course, dentists will learn the essential financial, operational, and strategic principles behind successful dental startups and practice acquisitions. We will uncover the 99 most common pitfalls that derail new owners before they ever open their doors—or during the first years of ownership. Attendees will explore how to evaluate opportunities, secure proper financing, and understand critical valuation metrics. The course explains the legal, tax, and compliance considerations involved in both startups and acquisitions. Participants will also learn how to identify red flags during due diligence and how to build systems that support profitability from day one. By the end, dentists will feel more confident navigating the complexities of ownership and avoiding costly, career-altering mistakes.
requirements: Identify major financial, legal, and operational pitfalls in startups and acquisitions Determine the viability of a dental practice opportunity using key data and benchmarks Examine practice valuation factors and understand their impact on purchase decisions Understand both benefits and risks of starting vs. buying a practice Recognize due-diligence findings to spot red flags before committing Create a structured action plan for opening or acquiring a practice Implement systems that support earl

title: Your Patients Use AI. Your Mom Uses AI. Do You?
description: The Artificial Intelligence conversation has shifted from “What can AI detect?” to “How does AI reshape operational excellence and clinical quality?” In this course, we will move beyond what anatomy and pathology dental AI can identify and quantify, into how can we leverage this technology to measure oral health, breakthrough CBCT applications, quality control of radiographic exposures by our clinical staff, and evidence based practice metrics to improve quality of care delivery.
requirements: Understand how dental AI models are developed Explore current and emerging applications that improve oral and overall health outcomes Identify opportunities for AI-assisted care to enhance diagnostic consistency, elevate the standard of care, and streamline clinical processes within private practice settings Evaluate real in-vivo clinical cases to assess AI model accuracy and learn how clinicians can use AI to strengthen patient communication and treatment acceptance

title: Opportunities with U.S. Army Dental Corps
description: In this course, we will be discussing opportunities that are available to dentists and future dentists that are interested in joining the Army. We will have an overview of our scholarship that pays for tuition, fees, and books as well as paying dental students a monthly stipend. We will also discuss opportunities for specialty training that are available to Army dentists. Qualified dentists that are interested in joining can take advantage of our accession bonus as well as our loan repayment. The Army is full of opportunity while doing the honorable service to treat those who sacrifice so much for our country.
requirements: Understand some of the opportunities available to dentists who are willing to join the US Army Utilize the knowledge of a current Army Dental Officer and Army medical recruiters as a resource for more information Compare some differences between a traditional private dental practice and a military practice Recognize opportunities with the Army Dental Corps are available to majority of dentists Determine if the Army is a potential option for them

title: AI for Dental Professionals: Transforming Patient Care and Clinical Confidence
description: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly reshaping how dental professionals assess, diagnose, and communicate with patients. This course breaks down the practical applications of AI in hygiene and clinical workflows - how it can help improve diagnostic consistency, enhance patient education, and reduce burnout through better efficiency. The instructor will share real-world examples of how to support hygienists and clinical teams to work more collaboratively and confidently. Attendees will gain a clearer understanding of how to integrate AI into their day-to-day workflow without losing the human touch that defines dentistry.
requirements: Understand the basics of the recent AI hype, how these tools work, common failure modes, and what to consider for privacy and accuracy Learn where AI fits today in hygiene and clinical workflows (and where it doesn’t) Discover practical ways to use AI for clearer patient communication without losing trust or tone Identify low-risk, high-value uses for AI in documentation and patient education, with quality checks

title: Thriving Not Surviving: Leading a Progressive Inflammation Management Program
description: In this course both dentists and hygienist's will learn the leadership roles for successful implementation of Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT) and developing a thriving inflammation program in their office. Participants will gain actionable insights and strategies to confidently lead their teams, optimize patient outcomes and transition from merely surviving to truly thriving in their approach to oral healthcare.
requirements: Understand the fundamental principles and benefits of Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT) Identify key leadership responsibilities for both dental hygienists and dentists in GBT implementation Develop a strategic plan for integrating GBT in existing work flows Implement effective communication strategies to enhance patient acceptance and compliance with GBT Measure and monitor the success of a progressive inflammation management

title: BLS (CPR) for Health Care Providers Certification: Skills Session FRI 3:30 PM
description: The American Heart Association: HeartCode® BLS CPR offers blended learning for Health Care professionals. Train online any time of day or night, then come to Yankee 2026 for your skills check session. This hands-on American Heart Association Health Care Provider-level CPR Renewal program reviews cardiopulmonary resuscitation, AED use, and choking emergencies in adults, children, and infants. Step 1: Sign up at Heartcode-BLS to take your interactive online training and the written exam. This online course is not administered through Yankee so there is a separate fee. Click this link to sign up online: https://shopcpr.heart.org/heartcode-bls Print your online certificate of training and bring it with you to Yankee. You MUST bring the printed certificate of successfully completing the online course and exam in order to complete the skills check and certification process. Step 2 : Choose your 15-minute BLS timeslot during the Yankee 2026 registration process. During your session you will complete a brief, one-to-one skills check with your very own CPR instructor. After successfully completing the skills check, you will receive your new AHA e-certificate via email.

title: Evidence Based Guidelines for the Use of Over the Counter Oral Care Prophylactic
description: In this course, an evidence-based framework will be presented to help answer important patient questions around constructing an oral care routine for optimizing oral health, including product selection, product use, and usage habits.
requirements: Discuss the evidence for different oral care actives to deliver different benefits Identify the evidence for combinations of therapy under compliant usage Understand the evidence for the impact of usage habits and practices on efficacy Create the best oral care routine based on individual patient needs

title: Protocols for Peri-implant Health: Why Implants and Tissues May Have Issues
description: Achieving and maintaining peri-implant health is crucial for successful implant therapy. Dental hygienists play a vital role not only in the maintenance of healthy implants but also in managing patients with periimplant disease. This course will cover the steps to achieve peri-implant health, the reasons behind implant complications, and strategies for managing peri-implant disease. To provide a comprehensive understanding of implant outcomes, the program will discuss peri-implant disease classification, disease surveillance, implant maintenance, surgical placement, bone grafting outcomes, prosthetic contours, anatomical considerations, and systemic factors.
requirements: Recognize the factors contributing to stable and esthetic implant outcomes Discuss the AAP classification for peri-implant disease and the instrumentation and procedures used in implant maintenance Understand the etiology and clinical presentation of problematic implants Assess various prosthetic designs that may hinder patient home care Learn anatomical concerns and systemic factors related to implant therapy Examine the application of 3-D radiographs in implant therapy

title: Prosthodontic Specialty Group Program
description: Designed by Specialists, Open to All Bringing the All-On-X Concept to Life with Digital Advancements This case-based lecture is designed to deepen their expertise in full-arch implant rehabilitation through the All-on-X treatment concept . With a strong emphasis on digital integration, the course explores how cutting-edge technologies, such as CBCT with use of intraoral scanners, 3D printing and photogrammetry, can enhance precision, predictability, and efficiency in clinical practice. We are going back to planning more and executing more efficiently. Through the case-based discussions and protocol-driven instruction, attendees will gain practical strategies for delivering immediate and long-term success in full-arch restorations using digital advancements in collaboration with dental laboratories and to gauge what we can do in practice.
requirements: Analyze the prosthodontic principles underlying the All-On-X treatment concept, including patient selection, biomechanical considerations, and prosthesis design Integrate advanced digital tools such as CBCT, intraoral scanners, digital guides, and photogrammetry into planning, executing and completing full-arch implant-supported restorations Assess the impact of digital workflows on clinical efficiency, accuracy of implant placement, and long-term prosthetic outcomes in All-On-X cases Develop co

FRIDAY, JANUARY 30TH: SCHEDULE 9:45 – 10:00 am Product Chat with Dash Medical Gloves* Speaker: April Hale (Dash Medical Gloves), Mike Schoen (Dash Medical Gloves) • In this chat, say goodbye to glove struggles and discover how breakthrough technology is delivering effortless application, enhanced comfort, and improved efficiency for today’s dental professionals, while transforming hand protection, one smooth move at a time. * These programs are located outside the Multi-Site classroom and do not provide CE credits. 10:00 – 11:00 am Inside the C-Suite: Growth, Innovation, and Integration in the Modern DSO Speaker: William Neuman (Group Dentistry Now), Alyssa Carlin (Simply Dental Management), Ryan Torresan (Mosaic Dental Collective), Dmitry Burshteyn (Progressive Dental Management), Adam Richichi (Dental Associates of Connecticut) Identify key strategic priorities driving growth and scalability in today's most successful dental support organizations (DSOs) Understand the cross-functional dynamics between operations, finance, marketing, and executive leadership in navigating industry challenges Evaluate current trends and best practices in recruitment, retention, and patient acquisition across DSO models Apply executive-level thinking to decision-making processes around integration, M&A, and practice culture alignment 11:00 – 11:15 am Product Chat with Genuine Business Advisors* Speaker: John Gleason (Genuine Business Advisors) • In this chat, gain essential insights into successfully navigating the sale of a dental practice in today's market. Drawing on over 30 years experience as a multiple business owner and a Certified Mergers & Acquisition with the national alliance of M & A advisors, the instructor will share proven strategies from recent successes in the sale and acquisition process in the dental industry. * These programs are located outside the Multi-Site classroom and do not provide CE credit

title: Inside the C-Suite: Growth, Innovation, and Integration in the Modern DSO
description: Join a powerhouse panel of C-suite leaders from top DSOs—CEOs, COOs, CFOs, and CMOs—as they provide a 360-degree view into how executive teams align strategy, scale operations, and foster innovation. From financial foresight and operational efficiencies to marketing strategies and clinical integration, this session will uncover how leading DSOs are navigating a rapidly evolving landscape. Whether you're a growing group or an established organization, gain actionable insights from the people shaping the future of dental care delivery.
instructors:
Mr. Adam Richichi
Mr. Ryan Torresan
Mr. Dmitry Burshteyn
Mr. William Neumann
Ms. Alyssa Carlin

title: Next Step After a Transition
description: In this course, attendees will gain insights into financial management, including understanding cash flow, running practice management reports, and reconciling accounts receivable. You'll learn the essential functions of your professional team, how to transition existing staff smoothly, and strategies for effective day-to-day operations. The course will also cover critical logistical elements, such as transferring computer leases, optimizing cash management, and adapting to your new responsibilities.
requirements: Understanding cash flow and running management reports Recognize the vital roles of your professional team during the transition Transition the staff, payroll, employee benefits and handbook considerations Reconcile accounts receivable and address your new financial policy Implement cash management day-to-day operations Outline key functions on day one of your practice after transition

title: The Role of Clinical Leadership in Growing EBITDA
description: This course will explore the crucial connection between strong clinical leadership and EBITDA growth. Panelists will discuss how empowered clinicians can influence operational efficiencies, optimize care delivery, and support business performance. The discussion will highlight how aligning clinical strategies with organizational goals leads to sustainable, ethical profitability.
instructors:
Ms. Sarah Alofs
Carrie Peterson, DDS
Aman Kaur, DMD
requirements: Define how clinical leadership directly impacts EBITDA Implement operational efficiency through clinical alignment to balance production goals with quality outcomes Utilize clinical KPIs and analytics to inform clinical decisions Recognize the value of provider engagement and retention to positively impact team performance and the bottom line Examine the intersection of patient care and profitability by avoiding the “Production vs. Care” conflict Create cross-department collaboration to develop

title: Cultivating Leadership in the Modern Dental Practice
description: Great dentistry goes beyond clinical skill - it thrives on leadership. In this course, attendees will explore what it means to lead effectively in a modern dental practice, starting with personal growth and expanding to team development and culture. Weather you're managing a team of five or fifty, this course will help you provide you applicable insights and practices to understand your leadership style, communicate with clarity, and create an environment where people perform at their best. Packed with takeaways, this course is your blueprint for becoming the kind of leader others want to follow.
requirements: Identify key traits of effective leaders and assess individual leadership styles Implement strategies to build trust and accountability within dental teams Explain vision and expectations clearly to align team efforts with practice goals Understand common leadership challenges in dental settings, including conflict resolution and staff motivation Develop a personal leadership growth plan to continue evolving beyond the session

title: Go It Alone or Go PE: Firsthand Perspectives
description: As the dental industry continues to evolve, more practice owners are asking the critical question “Should I continue to grow independently, or partner with private equity?” In this candid and insightful course, hear directly from a variety of leaders who have made the journey. Attendees will hear from those who have charted their own course and those who have joined forces with private equity partners. This course is tailored to DSO owners, group practice owners, emerging practice owners and anyone looking to grow their organization.
instructors:
Matt Sutton
Mike White
Mr. Virgil Ochoa
Mr. Mick Janness
requirements: Understand the opportunities, challenges and long-term implications of each path Identify key factors to consider when evaluating strategic growth options Examine personal stories and advice from experienced practice leaders Describe examples of operational, financial, and cultural changes through growth

title: Marketing Must Understand the Entire Funnel: How AI Reveals the True ROI
description: Everyone tracks cost per lead. But the most profitable dental groups know that true ROI is determined after the lead is created. This course dives into how mid- and lower-funnel performance — like speed to lead, schedule rate, show rate, treatment acceptance, and re-care — drive actual financial results. Learn how leading DSOs are integrating marketing across the full patient journey and using AI to identify leaks, drive efficiency, and boost case acceptance. In this course, attendees will explore how marketing leaders can become full-funnel strategists and how AI helps uncover blind spots, automate action, and drive true growth.
requirements: Identify the critical stages of the dental patient funnel that impact ROI beyond lead generation Interpret AI-generated insights to uncover hidden scheduling, follow-up, and treatment acceptance issues Distinguish between vanity metrics and true performance indicators that align with revenue goals Demonstrate how AI tools can automate follow-up, monitor call quality, and improve speed to lead Formulate a strategy for full-funnel marketing ownership that aligns with organizational growth objectiv

title: Quality Equipment Management as the Foundation of Patient Safety and Care
description: Dental groups and DSOs invest millions on their dental equipment, yet many struggle with equipment-related incidents, workflow disruptions, and unexpected costs. This session reveals how implementing robust equipment management processes directly translates to measurable improvements in patient care quality, safety outcomes, and operational efficiency. Attendees will discover the proven framework connecting equipment lifecycle management to patient care excellence, including real-world case studies demonstrating how proactive maintenance, standardized protocols, and data-driven decision making reduce equipment failures, minimize patient risk, and enhance clinical team confidence.
requirements: Develop an understanding of dental equipment management and its importance Understand the direct correlation between equipment reliability and performance metrics Determine the essential components of a quality-driven equipment management system Implement strategies for asset management to create cost savings Explain how to incorporate best practices into your day-to-day operations Create systems to sustain improvement and implementation of best practices

title: Digital Dentures: Profitability and Partnership Across the DSO Enterprise
description: As DSOs strive to optimize clinical and financial outcomes, digitizing removable prosthetics is rapidly emerging as a strategic imperative. This course will explore how leading DSOs are leveraging formulary lab partnerships, centralized workflows, and digital denture protocols to reduce overhead, enhance patient experience, and accelerate profitability. The session opens with a technical overview of digital denture workflows and case study insights from across the industry. It then transitions into a panel discussion featuring clinical, operational, and executive voices from top DSOs and industry partners. Attendees will gain practical guidance on scaling digital removals, navigating change management with providers, and evaluating lab and vendor relationships for sustainable growth. Whether you’re a clinical leader or business executive, this discussion will equip you with actionable strategies to modernize one of dentistry’s most stubbornly analog workflows.
instructors:
Mr. Toby Sexton
Melissa Wissinger, RDH
Patrick Mangan, DDS
Todd Davis, DDS
requirements: Describe how digital denture workflows differ from traditional analog methods in terms of efficiency, scalability, and patient experience Compare the key clinical and operational drivers that make removables the next frontier for digital transformation in DSOs Explain the impact of formulary partnerships on reducing cost variability and improving clinical standardization Recognize the role of provider engagement and change management in successfully transitioning to digital removable workflows L

title: Dermal Filler Live Patient Training Course

No other course offers microcannula training at the foundational level. FACES dermal filler training is designed to build your confidence as an injector and prepare you to go back to your practice ready to safely and confidently inject patients. All FACES courses are designed to save you time and money by not requiring multiple levels of training. Reinvigorate your practice by adding dermal filler treatments with the only Anatomically Based, Academically Rigorous, Scientifically-Driven® Seminar. Attendees will be: • Treating live patients • Each injector supplies their own model patient • Patient treatment is not "required" to receive total credit if the practitioner is present during the afternoon live-patient treatment phase Attendees are encouraged to bring additional staff members. Staff enrollment fee is $185 per staff member per full-day-event. Staff members may not inject. Yankee Customer Service will contact the registered dentist for the name of the staff member attending. All staff members must be registered for Yankee Dental Congress. *Staff is defined as any individual who functions in a supportive role to the dentist or medical doctor. Those registered as Staff cannot hold a degree and/or license in Massachusetts, nor any other State, which allows them to deliver facial injectables with or without supervision. For example, disqualified individuals include those who hold the following degrees: RN, MD, PA, NP. Only Massachusetts dental licensees are permitted to inject live patients, as per Massachusetts law. PLEASE NOTE: If you are a licensed dentist outside of Massachusetts, please contact Yankee Customer Service to register; Yankeedental@massdental.org. title: Dermal Filler Refresher
description: ATTENTION: This refresher course only covers theory and anatomy and does not include a hands-on treatment component. This is not a certification course. This refresher course is appropriate for practitioners with prior dermal filler training. Was your dermal filler training over a year ago? Do you need to build your injectable confidence? Have you already completed a dermal filler course, but know you need a “refresher”? All dedicated practitioners know that updates and reviews are critical. Join us for this comprehensive half-day presentation and bring your knowledge and skills up to date. REQUIRED: To enroll in these refresher courses, you must have completed at least six hours of the corresponding foundational course. The refresher course only covers theory and anatomy and does not include a hands-on treatment component. This is not a certification course. Proof of completion* of a foundational course that included live-patient injections is required for enrollment. *PLEASE NOTE: Proof of completion includes a scanned certificate of completion or CE credit printout from an approved PACE, CERP, or CME provider.
requirements: At the conclusion of this course the attendee should be able to: Describe the characteristics of hyaluronic acid fillers and how they affect duration Outline the current available HA dermal fillers and their clinical applications Give examples of facial anatomical danger zones and possible adverse events Describe how hyaluronidase is used and the current protocol for vascular occlusion

title: Botox® Refresher
description: ATTENTION: This refresher course only covers theory and anatomy and does not include a hands-on treatment component. This is not a certification course. Are you ready for an update? Have you already completed a Botox course, but feel you would benefit from a “refresher”? Was your last training over a year ago? Or maybe you completed training and still don’t feel confident? All dedicated practitioners know that updates and reviews are critical. Join us for this comprehensive didactic presentation and bring your knowledge, techniques, and skills up to date. REQUIRED: To enroll in these refresher courses, you must have completed at least six hours of the corresponding foundational course. Proof of completion* of a foundational course that included live-patient injections is required for enrollment. *PLEASE NOTE: Proof of completion includes a scanned certificate of completion or CE credit printout from an approved PACE, CERP, or CME provider.
requirements: At the conclusion of this course the attendee should be able to: Discuss the mode of action of all current FDA approved neurotoxins Estimate dosing ranges for muscles of facial expression and TMJ (masseter and temporalis) Describe storage, handling, and reconstitution for toxins

title: Spear Education: Treating the Terminal Dentition & Fully Edentulous Patient
description: Two Full Days: Thursday, January 29, 2026: 8:30 am – 12:30 pm; 1:30 – 4:30 pm AND Friday, January 30, 2026: 8:30 am – 12:30 pm; 1:30 – 4:30 pm The patient with terminal dentition or with an edentulous state unquestionably faces a disfiguring condition. Over the past decades, osseointegrated implants have provided extraordinary solutions, either supporting or retaining prosthetic devices aimed at restoring both form and function. This two-day course will explore the essentials of treatment planning and implant restorative design for the patient with terminal dentition and/or a fully edentulous condition. It will provide guidelines that can be easily implemented in practice in order to help interdisciplinary communication between the restorative dentist, the surgeon and the laboratory.

Ricardo Mitrani, DDS

title: Local Anesthesia Refresher Course
description: This course is offered to licensed, Registered Dental Hygienists who have already been certified in local anesthesia but feel they would benefit from instruction and review of injection landmarks and correct techniques. In addition, course instructors will share helpful suggestions for eliminating common errors and discuss how to avoid some post injection side-effects. Instructors will demonstrate proper clinical techniques through the use of various technologies. Participants will use a simulator to demonstrate correct needle placement. Since this course will be offered in Massachusetts, it is the responsibility of each attendee to understand the scope of practice for delivering local anesthesia in their respective state(s).
instructors:
Gwen Grosso, MS, RDH
Marie Paulis, RDH, MSDH

title: Not Your Average Dental Sealant
description: Dental sealants have long been proven to be a safe, effective and cost-effective preventive technique to help patients avoid occlusal decay in posterior teeth. Let’s talk about the different dental sealant material categories as well as the pros and cons of each. Although some practitioners have a favorite type of sealant material, it may not always be appropriate for all patients. In this hands on workshop, attendees will practice with several different sealant categories so you can be confident placing all dental sealants for your patients as needed.
requirements: Discuss the need for dental sealants Recognize the different dental sealant material categories Discover ways we can increase dental sealant usage Review the steps for placement of dental sealant materials Understand the need to follow the correct steps and sequence for each material category List the appropriate usage of each type of dental sealant material Identify options for retraction

title: Ergonomics in Dentistry: Protect Your Money Maker!
description: Working in dentistry doesn't have to hurt! Stop the aches and pains commonly associated with the practice of dentistry and dental hygiene. Protect your physical health with the use of ergonomic equipment, practical ergonomic strategies, and posture promoting exercises with specific stretches to reduce workplace pain. Experience this highly interactive program practices chairside stretches to establish a mind muscle connection for use in a daily release of chronically tight muscles.
requirements: Discuss risk factors for work related pain for the dental team Identify practical solutions for a correct workspace Explore strategies for comfortable practice that reduces fatigue and promotes safety Understand ergonomic equipment and how it works to prevent injury Discover posture enhancing strength exercises for injury prevention Implement workday stretches that can be done chairside for chronically tight muscles Handouts are available for this course: Download Now

title: Make a Great Impression
description: The goal of this course is to enable the team member to competently undertake dental impressions in a clinical environment for a range of activities including whitening trays, study models, and mouth guards. At the conclusion of the course, attendees will understand the benefits of an accurate impression and the consequences of an inaccurate impression. This course will also demonstrate the proper technique for model pouring and presentation to the patient. Attendees will practice taking impressions with alginate and alginate substitutes on a typodont and the instructors will be available to troubleshoot any problems.
requirements: Compare dental impressions in a clinical environment including whitening trays, study models, and mouth guards Discover the benefits of an accurate impression and the consequences of an inaccurate impression Demonstrate the proper technique for model pouring and presentation to the patient Practice taking impressions with alginate and alginate substitutes on a typodont Analyze alginate impressions to pinpoint and address any issues needing correction

description: This unique course combines science, live audience polling and clinical demonstrations to explore the expanding world of ultrasonic debridement for the treatment of oral inflammation. In this course, the instructor will focus on optimizing ultrasonic technology for the removal of biofilm, calculus and stain with emphasis placed on how to provide a positive patient experience while increasing the effectiveness and speed of a debridement procedure.

description: In this clinically relevant course, attendees will discuss a variety of ultrasonic debridement scenarios whereby clinicians often make decisions that negatively impact the removal of calculus and biofilm and create discomfort for patients. In addition, the instructor will provide practical solutions which can immediately be implemented into your dental hygiene appointments.
