requirements: Understand the role of digital dentistry and how it impacts patient care, efficiency, and team collaboration Perform digital scans with accuracy and confidence using modern intraoral scanners Manage digital files, organize scan data, and communicate effectively with labs and clinicians Identify opportunities to integrate same-day dentistry Recognize how assistants contribute to case preparation and execution Explore workflows involving whitening, aligners, restorative design, and digital smile s

title: Amplify Your Energy: Seven Ways to Elevate Your Stamina!
description: In this course attendees will explore practical strategies to support goals to alleviate fatigue, boost energy levels and enhance overall well-being. An evidence based approach to nutrient timing (fasting or 5 small meals?); busting the 8 glasses of water myth; superfoods vs the latest supplements; 8 hours of sleep or polyphasic sleeping; steady state exercise or sprint training. Bonus material on "reverse aging" will be shared.
requirements: Develop an understanding of the necessary nutrients and micronutrients for optimal living Discover how poor sleep habits are sabotaging your clinical goals Discuss how sarcopenia is a bigger enemy than osteopenia Understand how unresolved conflict burdens your "get up and go" energy Learn the insider secrets of the TOP PERSONAL TRAINERS to epigenetically turn your tired mitochondria back on

title: Six Ways to Create Equilibrium in A Helter-Skelter World
description: Hippocrates, 300 BC, said, “Disease starts in your gut.” There’s a complex link between your emotional well-being, the gut, and the 40 trillion single-celled organisms that call you home. Imbalances in your microbiome have been linked to mental health conditions like anxiety and depression. Poorly managed stress disrupts the balance of bacteria in your GI tract. Dietary choices, time zone travel, artificial sweeteners, sedentary living, and poor sleep also impact your trillions of little friends, which in turn affect your ability to regulate your emotions. Attend this insightful course and enjoy a novel path to achieving homeostasis.
requirements: Gain insight into the new science of your second brain, the 400 million neurons that make up your enteric nervous system in YOUR GUT Discover why NEW findings from the National Institute of Health about your gut flora are shaking the very foundation of health and well-being Understand how much your gut health (prebiotics/ probiotics/postbiotics) impacts your energy levels and emotional well-being Discuss how your emotions and thoughts impact your gut and how your gut bacteria impact your emotion

title: Case Planning and Occlusion for the Everyday Practice
description: Treatment planning and occlusion seem to get relegated and put on hold for those ‘special’ cases and perfect patients. In this course, we will explore and discuss the application of communication, treatment planning and occlusion in everyday dentistry and beyond. The instructor will take attendees on a guided tour of the application of these principles in a variety of cases from single tooth, quadrant and full mouth, to multidisciplinary care. Our goal is to help you implement these skills in your practice.
requirements: Understand purpose versus process treatment planning as it applies to outcome-based treatment planning Discuss the principles of facially-generated treatment planning Implement a simplified presentation protocol that patients will understand and appreciate Appreciate the benefits of a patient-centered, doctor-facilitated approach to case planning and acceptance Understand when, why, and how phasing treatment can be done safely

title: The Balance of Form and Function
description: Occlusion in training and application has been so over complicated that is difficult to understand and implement. During this course, the instructor will review the five foundational concepts of occlusion as they relates to our restorative planning and execution. Each concept will be supported in case application.

description: Empowering Dentists and Practice Leaders to Drive Growth, Culture, and Team Success This transformative program is designed for dentists and practice leaders who are ready to evolve from task managers to strategic leaders. Whether you are a doctor, office manager, or key stakeholder, this course equips you with the mindset and tools to operate your practice with the focus and clarity of a CEO.

With the increased need to reduce bacterial aerosols, Diode lasers can be a beneficial tool to help treat periodontal disease while reducing our patient’s bacterial flora during treatment. So, whether you are new to lasers, are looking to incorporate laser technology into your Hygiene program or are a current laser user, this course will provide realistic applications for everyday laser use.

title: From “Everyday” to Extraordinary: Level-Up Your Composite Skills
description: Dental materials and techniques have evolved significantly over the past 10 years. This course will cover materials and techniques for restoring class I’s, II’s and IV’s with all the tips that the instructor has learned over his 20 plus years of practicing dentistry. title: Let’s Talk Solutions! Uncovering, Addressing, and Treating Dry Mouth
description: The suffering caused by chronic dry mouth is vastly underestimated, often dismissed as a minor inconvenience rather than the serious health threat it truly is. Xerostomia or dryness of the oral cavity is now affecting 1 out of every 4 adults and has become one of the most common oral health pathologies. Advances in medicine prolonging lifespan, over 1100 drugs that are classified xerogenic, the growing use of CPAP machines, the rapid rise in diabetes, and HPV-driven head and neck cancers are among the contributing factors. The lack of saliva puts today’s patient in an extremely vulnerable state, significantly compromising their oral health, comfort, and overall well-being. The dental community have struggled to find effective, science-based solutions to offer to those who are suffering in silence until now. Be armed with transformative solutions to combat the silent struggle of xerostomia for so many of your patients. Walk away with the knowledge and tools to not just acknowledge, but truly address the devastating effects of xerostomia—transforming patient care and restoring quality of life. Your patients are counting on you.
requirements: Understand the prevalence and etiology of xerostomia related to today’s population Recognize the patient's perspective on living with chronic dry mouth Define normal salivary function versus xerostomic conditions Incorporate evaluative salivary assessment into clinical practice Effectively treat the xerostomic patient with innovative strategies to employ chairside and at home Discuss revolutionary approaches that will transform dry mouth care Handouts are available for this course: Download

title: Understanding the Mind of a Dentist: Why Are So Many of Us Unhappy?
description: So, you went to dental school so that you wouldn't have stress and long work hours? How's that working out for you? Dental offices and dentistry is a unique stressor to the mind and body. Come listen to the instructor breakdown every part of the office dysfunction in a comical way, you will be sure to laugh and cry. Every dentist has fears and insecurities that erode their quality of life and can lead to depression or early death. Dental staffs likewise share in this phenomenon, and often can feel overwhelmed or unappreciated. Your eyes will be opened to how all dentist offices and staffs face the same challenges and anxiety. The course will explore the inner workings of a dental office and its personnel. In this laugh-a-thon, the instructor will review all the crazies idiosyncrasies that dentists have. Take his patented 13 question quiz during the course to see if you suffer from mild moderate or severe anxiety. Bring the whole team and laugh, learn and grow into a dental team that doesn't let the minutia of daily dental stress get them down. Burn out of dentists and team members is on the rise across the country. Don't become cynical and start despising work.
requirements: Provide insights into why so many dentists are unhappy Discuss the daily stresses and pressures in running an office Review the jobs on the team and how often dysfunction exists Create a safe environment to laugh at how crazy this profession is

title: Curiosity Revived the Cat: How a Sense of Wonder Can Revive Your Career
description: Spilling the Tea: Secrets to Team Success! Attention, dental professionals! We all know what it feels like to be unfulfilled, lose sight of our purpose, or ready to throw in the towel. It's time to break free from those chains and unleash the power of our curiosity! Sure, as kids we were all curious little explorers, but as we aged, society conditioned us to believe our curiosity could lead to trouble. So, we stopped questioning and just went through the motions, sinking into the monotony of "getting it done." But let's be real: that's when we lose sight of ourselves, our passion, and our connection to our profession, teams, and patients. Well, no more! It's time to rekindle that natural sense of awe and wonder that got us here in the first place! In this course, attendees will delve into various techniques to manage stress, communicate effectively in challenging scenarios, use motivational interviewing techniques, and more. We will travel down the five pathways and explore our: self, profession, education, patients, and team. By tapping into our inherent sense of wonder, we'll not only ignite our passion for our work but also enhance the connection with our patients, team, and ourselves. So come along and discover how curiosity will revive you...just like the cat!
requirements: Identify actionable steps to elevate your career in dentistry Evaluate and enhance communication skills Investigate the power of “Why” and the patient experience Discuss ways to complete the stress cycle Apply the Awareness Wheel Model to confidently navigate difficult conversations *Tuition includes a coffee and tea break with an opportunity to network with peers. Handouts are available for this course: Download Now

title: The Modern Day Dental Exam: Digital Scanning to Clear Aligners

In the modern dental world, dentists can leverage the power of digital scanning to create an interactive digital environment where their patients can see their own teeth and understand their needs. Visualization is the key to acceptance. This course will describe how modern day dental exams are done, and then how to use clear aligners to create an optimal health situation for your patients.

title: Treating Aging America: Time to “Doctor Up!”
description: Clinical management of geriatric patients can be both blessed and challenging, especially as oral health can mean life-or death for them. The instructor learned this well through treating nursing facility patients for the last 30 years. When and how to safely employ restorative, surgical, preventive, and prosthetic techniques for ageing and medically complicated patients will be discussed.

Digital denture pros/cons, partial fabrication, reline hints, and immediate denture techniques, and an easy implant attachment substitution procedure will all be discussed. If you want to make your patients happy and cut your clinical time and efforts dramatically attend this lecture. Successful? Try 1022 patients, 1933 prostheses, and only 422 Total

title: Management of Traumatic Dental Injuries
description: Management of traumatic injuries is one of the most convoluted topics to comprehend for practicing general dentists and endodontists/other specialists alike. While the AAE and International Trauma Guidelines provide detailed information for traumatic injuries, using a structured framework by utilizing concept maps can simplify the comprehension of this highly significant topic. This course will discuss in detail the updated guidelines on management of traumatic dental injuries for permanent and deciduous dentition.
requirements: Classify traumatic dental injuries Evaluate and diagnose traumatic injuries Treat traumatic injuries according to AAE and International Trauma Guidelines Understand splinting times and techniques for various traumatic injuries Predict and manage potential sequelae of traumatic injuries Identify referral needs for patients

By the end of this course, participants will possess the knowledge and strategies needed to confidently prepare for and successfully manage Dental Board inspections.

title: Handling the Unexpected: Medical Emergency Management in Dental Practices
description: This course provides the knowledge and skills necessary to identify, respond to and manage various medical emergencies that may arise in a dental setting. Attendees will be equipped information on how to properly manage emergency situations confidently and efficiently, ensuring the safety and well-being of their patients.
requirements: Describe the common medical emergencies encountered in dental care and explain methods for evaluating and mitigating potential crises Demonstrate how to assess the severity of medical emergencies and apply appropriate emergency protocols Illustrate strategies for collaborating effectively with local emergency services Analyze specific scenarios to determine the most effective response and evaluate the outcomes of emergency interventions Summarize essential emergency equipment and medications use

title: Management of Immature Apex of Permanent Teeth
description: This course will provide a comprehensive overview of the biologic principles and clinical techniques used in the treatment of immature permanent teeth with necrotic or vital pulps. Attendees will learn to differentiate between traditional approaches such as Apexification and Apexogenesis and explore the latest advances in regenerative endodontic procedures (REPs). Emphasis will be placed on case selection, material choices, procedural protocols, and expected clinical outcomes, with a focus on evidence-based decision-making.

title: The Gum Game Plan
description: The Gum Game Plan™ is a case-based course designed for dental professionals who are ready to move beyond cookie-cutter treatment plans. Through real-life clinical scenarios, attendees will explore the art of asking the right questions, identifying key clinical findings, and aligning products, frequencies, and techniques to each patient’s unique needs. This interactive course equips clinicians with practical scripts, decision trees, and the confidence to customize soft tissue management strategies that deliver measurable results.
requirements: Analyze clinical case studies to determine the appropriate classification of periodontal or gingival conditions Formulate customized soft tissue management protocols based on patient history, risk factors, and clinical findings Interpret patient-reported concerns and correlate them with diagnostic markers to guide treatment recommendations Develop tailored homecare and in-office treatment plans using evidence-based modalities and delivery systems Demonstrate confident communication with patients

title: Dental Implants for Dental Assistants: Surgical and Restorative Foundations
description: This foundational course is designed to provide dental assistants with a comprehensive overview of dental implant procedures, equipping them with essential knowledge and skills to support both surgical and restorative phases. The course will explore the step-by-step process of dental implant placement, covering both freehand and guided surgical techniques. Attendees will gain insight into the components of surgical cassettes, key instrumentation, and critical auxiliaries necessary for efficient implant surgeries. Additionally, the course will delve into implant restoration techniques, including open tray, closed tray, and digital scanning workflows, preparing dental assistants to confidently assist in both analog and digital restorative procedures.

title: Keeping Up with Recent Dental Code Changes
description: Dental procedure codes change every year. Many practices miss billing and reporting opportunities to describe properly the services they are providing. In this course, the instructor will discuss new and recent codes, how to use them properly in the practice for improved claims success and which codes are being scrutinized and why. Peek into common insurance company policies and why certain codes have increased scrutiny. Understand utilization management basics. Eliminate common code mistakes that interfere with being paid. This course is great for the entire team to understand how best to report and get paid for what they do.
requirements: Examine the dental coding system and discuss how to use the various coding guides Compare new codes from the last several years and those that have been expired or deleted Discuss which codes are being scrutinized by payers and why and how to manage them Understand how utilization management is affecting the dental benefits industry Identify how to avoid common coding mistakes, how to report the services provided, and how to enhance payment for services rendered Handouts are available for this c

title: Secrets of Smoother Claims Processing: Top 10 Denial Reasons
description: Every year dentists continue to cite that working with dental insurance benefit programs is one of the main concerns of running their practices. This course discusses the ten top reasons claims are denied and what you can do to prevent delays in processing. Identify common office occurrences and errors that can interfere with payment, and how to enhance your office systems to smooth relationships with dental carriers. Attendees will learn how to improve an office's image with the insurance companies and identify what are your submissions telling the payers about your utilization pattern. We will welcome attendees to share some of their own common claims problems and discuss the growing concern of potential fraud, waste and abuse in insurance billing.
requirements: Review the most common reasons dental claims are denied Understand how to prevent common claims denial situations Implement chart documentation skills that will help support treatment planned and rendered Identify common errors in systems that can interfere with payment Discuss how to improve office image with insurance companies Discover how utilization patterns are being analyzed for potential fraud, waste, and abuse Handouts are available for this course: Download Now

title: GLP-1 Medications & the Dental Patient: Clinical Considerations for Oral Health
description: In this course, attendees will gain foundational knowledge of GLP-1 receptor agonist medications, including semaglutide, liraglutide, and related therapies, and their growing use in the management of type 2 diabetes and obesity. While direct research on the oral health implications of GLP-1s is currently limited, these medications are reshaping patient health in ways that intersect with dental care. Attendees will examine how GLP-1s affect systemic physiology, including metabolism, nutrition, bone density, and healing, factors with potential downstream relevance for the dental setting. The instructors will discuss observed changes in taste preferences, altered dietary patterns, and weight loss–related shifts that may influence caries risk, periodontal stability, and prosthodontic planning. The course aims to raise awareness, promote interprofessional communication, and equip dental teams to better understand and support patients using these increasingly common therapies.

Peyman Beigi, DMD
Nadine Tassabehji, PhD
Caroline Fox, MS
Ryan Kane, MD, MPH
